title.raw: 《酷吏列傳》 Biographies of Cruel Officials

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：「導之以政，齊之以刑，民免而無恥。

translations.literal: Confucius said: 'Guide them with government, regulate them with punishments, the people evade and have no shame.'
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said: "Lead the people with governmental measures and regulate them with punishments, and they will evade the law but feel no shame.

sentences.zh: 導之以德，齊之以禮，有恥且格。」

translations.literal: Guide them with virtue, regulate them with ritual, they have shame and moreover reform.
translations.idiomatic: Lead them with virtue and regulate them with propriety, and they will have a sense of shame and moreover reform themselves."

sentences.zh: 老氏稱：「上德不德，是以有德；

translations.literal: Laozi said: 'The highest virtue does not consider itself virtuous, therefore it has virtue;'
translations.idiomatic: Laozi said: "The highest virtue does not regard itself as virtuous, and therefore possesses true virtue;

sentences.zh: 下德不失德，是以無德。

translations.literal: The lowest virtue does not lose its virtue, therefore it has no virtue.
translations.idiomatic: The lowest virtue never loses sight of its own virtue, and therefore lacks true virtue.

sentences.zh: 法令滋章，盜賊多有。」

translations.literal: Laws and edicts multiply and grow, thieves and bandits are many.
translations.idiomatic: The more laws and edicts are promulgated, the more thieves and bandits arise."

sentences.zh: 太史公曰：信哉是言也！

translations.literal: The Grand Historian says: True indeed are these words!
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Historian remarks: How true these words are!

sentences.zh: 法令者治之具，而非制治清濁之源也。

translations.literal: Laws and edicts are tools of governance, but not the source that determines the purity or corruption of governance.
translations.idiomatic: Laws and edicts are merely the instruments of governance, not the wellspring that determines whether rule is pure or corrupt.

sentences.zh: 昔天下之網嘗密矣，然姦偽萌起，其極也，上下相遁，至於不振。

translations.literal: In the past the net of the realm was once tight, yet treachery and fraud sprouted; at its extreme, superiors and subordinates deceived each other, reaching the point of decline.
translations.idiomatic: In former times, the net of laws across the realm was drawn ever tighter, yet fraud and treachery continued to sprout. Carried to its extreme, those above and below deceived one another until the whole order fell into decline.

sentences.zh: 當是之時，吏治若救火揚沸，非武健嚴酷，惡能勝其任而愉快乎！

translations.literal: At that time, official governance was like putting out fire by fanning boiling water; without the forceful, severe, and cruel, how could one bear the burden and find satisfaction!
translations.idiomatic: In those times, governance was like trying to douse a fire while fanning the flames — without officials who were forceful, severe, and ruthless, who could have shouldered such a burden and taken satisfaction in the task?

sentences.zh: 言道德者，溺其職矣。

translations.literal: Those who speak of morality and virtue drown in their duties.
translations.idiomatic: Those who preached morality and virtue were already drowning in their own offices.

sentences.zh: 故曰「聽訟，吾猶人也，必也使無訟乎」。

translations.literal: Therefore it is said: 'In hearing lawsuits, I am like other people; what is necessary is to bring about a state where there are no lawsuits.'
translations.idiomatic: Hence the saying: "In hearing lawsuits, I am no different from anyone else. What truly matters is to bring about a world where there are no lawsuits at all."

sentences.zh: 「下士聞道大笑之」。

translations.literal: 'When the lowest type of scholar hears the Way, he laughs loudly at it.'
translations.idiomatic: "When the lowest sort of scholar hears of the Way, he bursts out laughing."

sentences.zh: 非虛言也。

translations.literal: These are not empty words.
translations.idiomatic: These are no empty words.

sentences.zh: 漢興，破觚而為圜，斲雕而為樸，網漏於吞舟之魚，而吏治烝烝，不至於姦，黎民艾安。

translations.literal: When the Han arose, it broke the angular and made it round, cut away the carved and made it plain; the net leaked fish large enough to swallow boats, yet governance steadily progressed, did not reach corruption, and the common people lived in peace.
translations.idiomatic: When the Han dynasty arose, it rounded off the sharp edges and smoothed the ornate back to simplicity. The net of laws was so loose that fish large enough to swallow boats could slip through, yet governance improved steadily, corruption never took hold, and the common people lived in peace.

sentences.zh: 由是觀之，在彼不在此。

translations.literal: Viewed from this, it lies in that and not in this.
translations.idiomatic: Viewed from this perspective, the key lies in that approach, not this one.

sentences.zh: 高后時，酷吏獨有侯封，刻轢宗室，侵辱功臣。

translations.literal: In the time of Empress Dowager Gao, the only harsh official was Hou Feng, who oppressed the imperial clan and humiliated meritorious officials.
translations.idiomatic: During the reign of Empress Dowager Gao, the only harsh official was Hou Feng, who ground down the members of the imperial clan and humiliated ministers of merit.

sentences.zh: 呂氏已敗，遂（禽）［夷］侯封之家。

translations.literal: After the Lu clan was defeated, they then exterminated Hou Feng's family.
translations.idiomatic: After the downfall of the Lu clan, Hou Feng's entire family was wiped out.

sentences.zh: 孝景時，晁錯以刻深頗用術輔其資，而七國之亂，發怒於錯，錯卒以被戮。

translations.literal: In the time of Emperor Jing, Chao Cuo used harsh severity and various stratagems to augment his authority; when the Rebellion of the Seven Kingdoms erupted, anger was directed at Cuo, and Cuo was ultimately executed.
translations.idiomatic: In the reign of Emperor Jing, Chao Cuo relied on harsh severity and political stratagems to bolster his position. When the Rebellion of the Seven Kingdoms broke out, fury was turned upon him, and Cuo was put to death.

sentences.zh: 其后有郅都、寧成之屬。

translations.literal: After him there were the likes of Zhi Du and Ning Cheng.
translations.idiomatic: After him came the likes of Zhi Du and Ning Cheng.

sentences.zh: 郅都者，楊人也。

translations.literal: Zhi Du was a man from Yang.
translations.idiomatic: Zhi Du was a native of Yang.

sentences.zh: 以郎事孝文帝。

translations.literal: He served Emperor Wen as a Gentleman.
translations.idiomatic: He served Emperor Wen as a Gentleman of the court.

sentences.zh: 孝景時，都為中郎將，敢直諫，面折大臣於朝。

translations.literal: In the time of Emperor Jing, Du served as Commander of the Gentlemen of the Palace, dared to remonstrate directly, and openly rebuked great ministers at court.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Jing, Zhi Du became Commander of the Gentlemen of the Palace. He dared to speak his mind bluntly and would rebuke the highest ministers to their faces at court.

sentences.zh: 嘗從入上林，賈姬如廁，野彘卒入廁。

translations.literal: Once when accompanying the emperor into the Shanglin Park, Lady Jia went to the privy, and a wild boar suddenly burst into the privy.
translations.idiomatic: Once, while accompanying the emperor on a visit to the Shanglin Park, Lady Jia went to use the privy, and a wild boar suddenly charged in after her.

sentences.zh: 上目都，都不行。

translations.literal: The emperor signaled Du with his eyes, but Du did not move.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor glanced at Zhi Du, but Zhi Du did not stir.

sentences.zh: 上欲自持兵救賈姬，都伏上前曰：「亡一姬復一姬進，天下所少寧賈姬等乎？

translations.literal: The emperor wanted to take up weapons himself to save Lady Jia, but Du prostrated himself before the emperor and said: 'Lose one consort and another will come forward; does the empire lack the likes of Lady Jia?'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor was about to seize a weapon and rush to Lady Jia's rescue, but Zhi Du threw himself at the emperor's feet and said: "Lose one consort and another will take her place. Is the empire so short of women like Lady Jia?

sentences.zh: 陛下縱自輕，柰宗廟太后何！」

translations.literal: Even if Your Majesty thinks little of yourself, what about the ancestral temples and the Empress Dowager!
translations.idiomatic: Even if Your Majesty holds his own life cheap, what of the ancestral temples and the Empress Dowager?"

sentences.zh: 上還，彘亦去。

translations.literal: The emperor turned back, and the boar also left.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor drew back, and the boar, too, departed.

sentences.zh: 太后聞之，賜都金百斤，由此重郅都。

translations.literal: When the Empress Dowager heard of this, she bestowed a hundred catties of gold upon Du, and from this time on she held Zhi Du in high esteem.
translations.idiomatic: When the Empress Dowager heard of this, she rewarded Zhi Du with a hundred catties of gold, and from that time on held him in the highest regard.

sentences.zh: 濟南瞯氏宗人三百餘家，豪猾，二千石莫能制，於是景帝乃拜都為濟南太守。

translations.literal: The Jian clan of Jinan had over three hundred households; they were powerful and lawless, and no two-thousand-picul official could control them. So Emperor Jing appointed Du as Grand Administrator of Jinan.
translations.idiomatic: The Jian clan of Jinan comprised more than three hundred households. They were powerful and lawless, and no administrator of two-thousand-picul rank had been able to bring them to heel. Emperor Jing therefore appointed Zhi Du as Grand Administrator of Jinan.

sentences.zh: 至則族瞯氏首惡，餘皆股栗。

translations.literal: Upon arriving he executed the entire clan of the Jian chief offenders; the rest all trembled with fear.
translations.idiomatic: Upon his arrival, he executed the ringleaders of the Jian clan and their families. The rest of the populace trembled in terror.

sentences.zh: 居歲餘，郡中不拾遺。

translations.literal: After a year and more, no one in the commandery picked up lost items on the road.
translations.idiomatic: Within a little over a year, no one in the commandery so much as picked up things dropped on the road.

sentences.zh: 旁十餘郡守畏都如大府。

translations.literal: The governors of more than ten neighboring commanderies feared Du as they would a superior.
translations.idiomatic: The governors of more than ten neighboring commanderies feared Zhi Du as though he were their own superior.

sentences.zh: 都為人勇，有氣力，公廉，不發私書，問遺無所受，請寄無所聽。

translations.literal: Du was brave as a person, with spirit and strength; he was fair and incorruptible, never opened private letters, accepted no gifts or presents, and listened to no personal requests.
translations.idiomatic: Zhi Du was a man of courage and vigor. He was scrupulously fair and incorruptible — he never opened private letters, accepted no gifts, and refused all personal entreaties.

sentences.zh: 常自稱曰：「已倍親而仕，身固當奉職死節官下，終不顧妻子矣。」

translations.literal: He often said of himself: 'Having turned my back on family to serve in office, I should devote myself to duty and die for principle at my post, never looking back at wife and children.'
translations.idiomatic: He was fond of saying: "Having turned my back on my parents to serve in office, I must devote myself to duty and, if need be, die for principle at my post. I shall never look back for the sake of wife and children."

sentences.zh: 郅都遷為中尉。

translations.literal: Zhi Du was promoted to Commandant of Justice.
translations.idiomatic: Zhi Du was promoted to the post of Commandant of Justice.

sentences.zh: 丞相條侯至貴倨也，而都揖丞相。

translations.literal: The Chancellor, the Marquis of Tiao, was extremely noble and proud, yet Du merely bowed to the Chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: The Chancellor, the Marquis of Tiao, was a man of the highest rank and considerable pride, yet Zhi Du greeted the Chancellor with nothing more than a casual bow.

sentences.zh: 是時民樸，畏罪自重，而都獨先嚴酷，致行法不避貴戚，列侯宗室見都側目而視，號曰「蒼鷹」。

translations.literal: At this time the people were simple and feared punishment and valued themselves; yet Du alone led with severity and cruelty, enforcing the law without sparing the noble or the imperial relatives. The marquises and members of the imperial clan looked at Du with sidelong glances, calling him 'the Grey Hawk.'
translations.idiomatic: At that time the people were simple and law-abiding, cautious for fear of punishment, yet Zhi Du alone took the lead in ruthless severity, enforcing the law without sparing the noble or the wellborn. The marquises and members of the imperial clan could only glare at him from the corners of their eyes, and they gave him the name "the Grey Hawk."

sentences.zh: 臨江王徵詣中尉府對簿，臨江王欲得刀筆為書謝上，而都禁吏不予。

translations.literal: The King of Linjiang was summoned to the Commandant's office to answer charges; the King of Linjiang wished to obtain a writing knife and brush to compose a letter of apology to the emperor, but Du ordered his clerks not to provide them.
translations.idiomatic: When the King of Linjiang was summoned to the Commandant's office to answer charges, he wished to obtain a brush and writing knife to compose a letter of apology to the emperor, but Zhi Du forbade his clerks from providing them.

sentences.zh: 魏其侯使人以閒與臨江王。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Weiqi sent someone to secretly provide them to the King of Linjiang.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Weiqi secretly sent someone to slip the writing materials to the King of Linjiang.

sentences.zh: 臨江王既為書謝上，因自殺。

translations.literal: After the King of Linjiang wrote a letter of apology to the emperor, he then killed himself.
translations.idiomatic: After the King of Linjiang had written his letter of apology to the emperor, he took his own life.

sentences.zh: 竇太后聞之，怒，以危法中都，都免歸家。

translations.literal: When Empress Dowager Dou heard of this, she was furious and used threatening legal charges against Du; Du was dismissed and returned home.
translations.idiomatic: When Empress Dowager Dou learned of this, she was enraged and had capital charges brought against Zhi Du. He was dismissed from office and sent home.

sentences.zh: 孝景帝乃使使持節拜都為鴈門太守，而便道之官，得以便宜從事。

translations.literal: Emperor Jing then sent an envoy bearing the imperial tally to appoint Du as Grand Administrator of Yanmen, traveling directly to his post, with authority to act as he saw fit.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Jing then dispatched an envoy bearing the imperial tally to appoint Zhi Du as Grand Administrator of Yanmen, sending him directly to his post with full authority to act at his own discretion.

sentences.zh: 匈奴素聞郅都節，居邊，為引兵去，竟郅都死不近鴈門。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu had long heard of Zhi Du's integrity; when he was stationed on the border, they withdrew their troops, and for the entire time until Zhi Du's death they did not approach Yanmen.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu had long known of Zhi Du's formidable reputation. When he was stationed on the frontier, they pulled their forces back and, for the entire duration until Zhi Du's death, did not dare approach Yanmen.

sentences.zh: 匈奴至為偶人象郅都，令騎馳射莫能中，見憚如此。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu even made a wooden figure in the likeness of Zhi Du and had their horsemen gallop past shooting at it, but none could hit it — so great was the dread he inspired.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu even fashioned a wooden effigy in the likeness of Zhi Du and ordered their horsemen to gallop past shooting at it, yet none could hit the mark — so profoundly did he terrify them.

sentences.zh: 匈奴患之。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu regarded him as a menace.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu considered him a dire threat.

sentences.zh: 竇太后乃竟中都以漢法。

translations.literal: Empress Dowager Dou then ultimately convicted Du under Han law.
translations.idiomatic: In the end, Empress Dowager Dou had Zhi Du convicted under the laws of Han.

sentences.zh: 景帝曰：「都忠臣。」

translations.literal: Emperor Jing said: 'Du is a loyal subject.'
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Jing protested: "Zhi Du is a loyal minister."

sentences.zh: 欲釋之。

translations.literal: He wished to release him.
translations.idiomatic: He wished to have him pardoned.

sentences.zh: 竇太后曰：「臨江王獨非忠臣邪？」

translations.literal: Empress Dowager Dou said: 'Was the King of Linjiang alone not a loyal subject?'
translations.idiomatic: But Empress Dowager Dou retorted: "Was the King of Linjiang not a loyal subject as well?"

sentences.zh: 於是遂斬郅都。

translations.literal: Thereupon Zhi Du was executed.
translations.idiomatic: And so Zhi Du was put to death.

sentences.zh: 寧成者，穰人也。

translations.literal: Ning Cheng was a man from Rang.
translations.idiomatic: Ning Cheng was a native of Rang.

sentences.zh: 以郎謁者事景帝。

translations.literal: He served Emperor Jing as a Gentleman Usher.
translations.idiomatic: He served Emperor Jing as a Gentleman Usher.

sentences.zh: 好氣，為人小吏，必陵其長吏；

translations.literal: He was hot-tempered; when he was a minor clerk, he invariably bullied his superiors;
translations.idiomatic: He was a man of fierce temper. When serving as a minor clerk, he invariably bullied his superiors;

sentences.zh: 為人上，操下如束溼薪。

translations.literal: When he was someone's superior, he controlled those below him like binding wet firewood.
translations.idiomatic: when placed in authority over others, he gripped his subordinates as tightly as one binds wet firewood.

sentences.zh: 滑賊任威。

translations.literal: He was cunning and treacherous, relying on his authority.
translations.idiomatic: He was cunning and ruthless, wielding his authority without scruple.

sentences.zh: 稍遷至濟南都尉，而郅都為守。

translations.literal: He was gradually promoted to Commandant of Jinan, while Zhi Du was the Governor.
translations.idiomatic: He rose through the ranks to become Commandant of Jinan, at a time when Zhi Du was serving as Governor there.

sentences.zh: 始前數都尉皆步入府，因吏謁守如縣令，其畏郅都如此。

translations.literal: Previously, the several Commandants had all walked into the Governor's office and, relying on their clerks, paid respects to the Governor as a county magistrate would; such was their fear of Zhi Du.
translations.idiomatic: Before Ning Cheng's arrival, every previous Commandant had entered the Governor's office on foot and, through their clerks, paid their respects to the Governor as deferentially as a county magistrate would — so greatly did they fear Zhi Du.

sentences.zh: 及成往，直陵都出其上。

translations.literal: When Cheng went there, he directly dominated Du, placing himself above him.
translations.idiomatic: But when Ning Cheng arrived, he brazenly rode roughshod over Zhi Du, asserting himself as the superior.

sentences.zh: 都素聞其聲，於是善遇，與結驩。

translations.literal: Du had long heard of his reputation, and so treated him well and formed a friendship with him.
translations.idiomatic: Zhi Du, who had long heard of his reputation, therefore treated him with courtesy and struck up a friendship.

sentences.zh: 久之，郅都死，后長安左右宗室多暴犯法，於是上召寧成為中尉。

translations.literal: After some time, Zhi Du died; afterwards, many members of the imperial clan in and around Chang'an committed violent crimes, so the emperor summoned Ning Cheng to be Commandant of Justice.
translations.idiomatic: Some time later, Zhi Du died. Thereafter, many members of the imperial clan in and around Chang'an committed brazen violations of the law, and the emperor summoned Ning Cheng to serve as Commandant of Justice.

sentences.zh: 其治效郅都，其廉弗如，然宗室豪桀皆人人惴恐。

translations.literal: His governance imitated Zhi Du, though his integrity did not match; nevertheless, the powerful members of the imperial clan all trembled with fear.
translations.idiomatic: His methods of governance followed Zhi Du's example, though in integrity he fell short. Even so, every powerful figure in the imperial clan trembled with dread.

sentences.zh: 武帝即位，徙為內史。

translations.literal: When Emperor Wu ascended the throne, he was transferred to the post of Prefect of the Capital.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Wu came to the throne, Ning Cheng was transferred to the post of Prefect of the Capital.

sentences.zh: 外戚多毀成之短，抵罪髡鉗。

translations.literal: The imperial in-laws frequently slandered Cheng's faults, and he was convicted and sentenced to shaving and iron collar.
translations.idiomatic: The imperial in-laws frequently denounced his failings, and he was convicted and sentenced to having his head shaved and wearing an iron collar.

sentences.zh: 是時九卿罪死即死，少被刑，而成極刑，自以為不復收，於是解脫，詐刻傳出關歸家。

translations.literal: At that time, when ministers of the Nine Ministers rank were found guilty of capital crimes, they died; few suffered mutilating punishments. Yet Cheng received the severest punishment, and believing he would never be reinstated, he broke free, forged a travel pass, and slipped through the frontier gate to return home.
translations.idiomatic: In those days, when ministers of the Nine Ministers rank were convicted of capital offenses, they were executed — few suffered the humiliation of corporal punishment. Yet Ning Cheng received the harshest sentence. Convinced he would never be restored to office, he broke free of his bonds, forged a travel pass, and slipped through the frontier gate to return home.

sentences.zh: 稱曰：「仕不至二千石，賈不至千萬，安可比人乎！」

translations.literal: He declared: 'If one does not reach two-thousand-picul rank in office, or ten million cash in trade, how can one compare oneself to others!'
translations.idiomatic: He declared: "If a man cannot rise to two-thousand-picul rank in office, or amass ten million cash in trade, how can he hold his head up among his peers?"

sentences.zh: 乃貰貸買陂田千餘頃，假貧民，役使數千家。

translations.literal: He then borrowed on credit to buy over a thousand qing of irrigated farmland, leased it to poor people, and employed several thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: He borrowed on credit to purchase over a thousand qing of irrigated farmland, leased it to the poor, and put several thousand households to work.

sentences.zh: 數年，會赦。

translations.literal: After several years, an amnesty was declared.
translations.idiomatic: After several years, a general amnesty was proclaimed.

sentences.zh: 致產數千金，為任俠，持吏長短，出從數十騎。

translations.literal: He accumulated a fortune of several thousand catties of gold, acted as a knight-errant, held officials' faults and merits in his hand, and went about accompanied by dozens of horsemen.
translations.idiomatic: He amassed a fortune of several thousand catties of gold, styled himself a knight-errant, held leverage over officials by knowing their secrets, and rode out attended by dozens of horsemen.

sentences.zh: 其使民威重於郡守。

translations.literal: His authority over the people was greater than that of the commandery governor.
translations.idiomatic: His hold over the common people surpassed even that of the commandery governor.

sentences.zh: 周陽由者，其父趙兼以淮南王舅父侯周陽，故因姓周陽氏。

translations.literal: Zhouyang You: his father Zhao Jian was enfeoffed at Zhouyang because he was the maternal uncle of the King of Huainan, and so the family took the surname Zhouyang.
translations.idiomatic: Zhouyang You's father, Zhao Jian, had been enfeoffed at Zhouyang on account of being the maternal uncle of the King of Huainan, and the family thereafter took Zhouyang as their surname.

sentences.zh: 由以宗家任為郎，事孝文及景帝。

translations.literal: You was appointed Gentleman through the privilege of his noble house, and served Emperors Wen and Jing.
translations.idiomatic: Through the privilege of his noble house, Zhouyang You was appointed a Gentleman and served both Emperor Wen and Emperor Jing.

sentences.zh: 景帝時，由為郡守。

translations.literal: In Emperor Jing's time, You served as a commandery governor.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Jing, Zhouyang You served as a commandery governor.

sentences.zh: 武帝即位，吏治尚循謹甚，然由居二千石中，最為暴酷驕恣。

translations.literal: When Emperor Wu ascended the throne, official governance still adhered to caution and care; yet among the two-thousand-picul officials, You was the most violent, cruel, arrogant, and willful.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Wu came to the throne, the administration still prized caution and diligence. Yet among all the officials of two-thousand-picul rank, Zhouyang You was the most violent, cruel, arrogant, and self-indulgent.

sentences.zh: 所愛者，撓法活之；

translations.literal: Those he favored, he would bend the law to save;
translations.idiomatic: Those he favored, he would bend the law to save;

sentences.zh: 所憎者，曲法誅滅之。

translations.literal: Those he hated, he would twist the law to destroy.
translations.idiomatic: those he despised, he would twist the law to destroy.

sentences.zh: 所居郡，必夷其豪。

translations.literal: In whichever commandery he governed, he would invariably destroy the powerful families.
translations.idiomatic: In whichever commandery he governed, he invariably crushed the local strongmen.

sentences.zh: 為守，視都尉如令。

translations.literal: When he served as Governor, he treated the Commandant as a county magistrate.
translations.idiomatic: When serving as Governor, he treated the Commandant like a mere county magistrate.

sentences.zh: 為都尉，必陵太守，奪之治。

translations.literal: When he served as Commandant, he invariably dominated the Grand Administrator and seized control of governance.
translations.idiomatic: When serving as Commandant, he invariably rode roughshod over the Grand Administrator and seized control of affairs.

sentences.zh: 與汲黯俱為忮，司馬安之文惡，俱在二千石列，同車未嘗敢均茵伏。

translations.literal: He was as aggressive as Ji An, and as venomous in written attacks as Sima An; both were in the two-thousand-picul rank, yet when sharing a carriage no one dared share the cushion equally with him.
translations.idiomatic: He was as headstrong as Ji An and as venomous in his written denunciations as Sima An. Though all of them held the same two-thousand-picul rank, when riding in the same carriage no one dared share the cushion as an equal.

sentences.zh: 由后為河東都尉，時與其守勝屠公爭權，相告言罪。

translations.literal: Later, You served as Commandant of Hedong, and at that time fought for power with the Governor Shengtu Gong, each accusing the other of crimes.
translations.idiomatic: Later, Zhouyang You served as Commandant of Hedong, where he clashed with the Governor, Shengtu Gong, over power. Each accused the other of crimes.

sentences.zh: 勝屠公當抵罪，義不受刑，自殺，而由棄市。

translations.literal: Shengtu Gong was found guilty, but considering it beneath his honor to submit to punishment, he killed himself; You was executed in the marketplace.
translations.idiomatic: Shengtu Gong was found guilty but, considering it beneath his dignity to submit to punishment, took his own life. Zhouyang You, for his part, was executed in the marketplace.

sentences.zh: 自寧成、周陽由之后，事益多，民巧法，大抵吏之治類多成、由等矣。

translations.literal: After Ning Cheng and Zhouyang You, affairs multiplied, the people grew cunning with the law, and for the most part officials who governed were of the same type as Cheng and You.
translations.idiomatic: After the time of Ning Cheng and Zhouyang You, affairs grew ever more complex and the people more adept at exploiting the law. On the whole, the officials who governed were cut from the same cloth as Cheng and You.

sentences.zh: 趙禹者，斄人。

translations.literal: Zhao Yu was a man from Tai.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Yu was a native of Tai.

sentences.zh: 以佐史補中都官，用廉為令史，事太尉亞夫。

translations.literal: He entered service as an Assistant Clerk and was appointed to the Central Capital offices; because of his integrity he was made Chief Clerk, serving Grand Commandant Yafu.
translations.idiomatic: He entered government service as an Assistant Clerk assigned to the Central Capital offices. On account of his integrity, he was promoted to Chief Clerk and served under Grand Commandant Yafu.

sentences.zh: 亞夫為丞相，禹為丞相史，府中皆稱其廉平。

translations.literal: When Yafu became Chancellor, Yu served as Chancellor's Clerk; everyone in the office praised his integrity and fairness.
translations.idiomatic: When Yafu became Chancellor, Zhao Yu served as the Chancellor's Clerk, and everyone in the office praised him for his integrity and fairness.

sentences.zh: 然亞夫弗任，曰：「極知禹無害，然文深，不可以居大府。」

translations.literal: Yet Yafu did not promote him, saying: 'I know full well that Yu is blameless, but his writing is excessively severe — he cannot be placed in a high office.'
translations.idiomatic: Yet Yafu refused to promote him, saying: "I know perfectly well that Zhao Yu is without fault, but his legal writing is excessively severe. He is not fit for a position of high authority."

sentences.zh: 今上時，禹以刀筆吏積勞，稍遷為御史。

translations.literal: In the present emperor's time, Yu accumulated merit as a secretarial clerk and was gradually promoted to Censor.
translations.idiomatic: Under the present emperor, Zhao Yu accumulated merit through years of clerical work and was gradually promoted to the rank of Censor.

sentences.zh: 上以為能，至太中大夫。

translations.literal: The emperor considered him capable and promoted him to Grand Palace Grandee.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor considered him capable and raised him to the rank of Grand Palace Grandee.

sentences.zh: 與張湯論定諸律令，作見知，吏傳得相監司。

translations.literal: Together with Zhang Tang he deliberated and codified various laws and edicts, created the 'obligation to report' statute, so that officials in succession could supervise and check one another.
translations.idiomatic: Together with Zhang Tang, he deliberated on and codified the various laws and edicts. They created the statute of "obligation to report," whereby officials at every level could monitor and check one another.

sentences.zh: 用法益刻，蓋自此始。

translations.literal: The application of the law became ever more severe, and it was from this that it began.
translations.idiomatic: The ever-harsher application of the law may be traced to this very beginning.

sentences.zh: 張湯者，杜人也。

translations.literal: Zhang Tang was a man from Du.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Tang was a native of Du.

sentences.zh: 其父為長安丞，出，湯為兒守舍。

translations.literal: His father served as Vice Prefect of Chang'an; when he went out, Tang as a child was left to watch the house.
translations.idiomatic: His father served as the Vice Prefect of Chang'an. One day, when his father went out, the young Tang was left to mind the house.

sentences.zh: 還而鼠盜肉，其父怒，笞湯。

translations.literal: Upon returning, a rat had stolen the meat; his father was angry and whipped Tang.
translations.idiomatic: When the father returned and found that a rat had stolen the meat, he was furious and whipped the boy.

sentences.zh: 湯掘窟得盜鼠及餘肉，劾鼠掠治，傳爰書，訊鞫論報，并取鼠與肉，具獄磔堂下。

translations.literal: Tang dug into the burrow and found the thieving rat and the remaining meat; he indicted the rat and tortured it in interrogation, composed the official legal documents, questioned and tried it and pronounced judgment, then took the rat and the meat and publicly dismembered the rat beneath the hall.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Tang dug into the burrow, recovered the thieving rat and the remaining meat, drew up a formal indictment, subjected the rat to interrogation under torture, prepared the legal documents, conducted the trial, and pronounced his verdict. Then he took the rat and the meat and had the rat publicly dismembered beneath the hall.

sentences.zh: 其父見之，視其文辭如老獄吏，大驚，遂使書獄。

translations.literal: When his father saw this and examined his legal writings, which read as though written by a veteran prison clerk, he was greatly astonished and thenceforth set him to writing legal cases.
translations.idiomatic: When his father examined the boy's legal writings and found them as polished as a veteran prison clerk's, he was astonished and thenceforth put him to work drafting legal cases.

sentences.zh: 父死后，湯為長安吏，久之。

translations.literal: After his father died, Tang served as a clerk in Chang'an for a long time.
translations.idiomatic: After his father's death, Zhang Tang served for many years as a clerk in Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 周陽侯始為諸卿時，嘗系長安，湯傾身為之。

translations.literal: When the Marquis of Zhouyang first served as one of the ministers, he was once imprisoned in Chang'an, and Tang devoted himself wholeheartedly to his case.
translations.idiomatic: When the Marquis of Zhouyang first served as one of the ministers, he was once imprisoned in Chang'an. Zhang Tang devoted himself wholeheartedly to his case.

sentences.zh: 及出為侯，大與湯交，遍見湯貴人。

translations.literal: When he was released and enfeoffed as a marquis, he formed a close friendship with Tang and introduced Tang to all his noble connections.
translations.idiomatic: After his release and ennoblement as a marquis, he formed a close friendship with Zhang Tang and introduced him to all the influential men in his circle.

sentences.zh: 湯給事內史，為寧成掾，以湯為無害，言大府，調為茂陵尉，治方中。

translations.literal: Tang was attached to the Prefect of the Capital; he served as a clerk under Ning Cheng. Considering Tang to be blameless, Ning Cheng recommended him to the central government, and he was transferred to serve as Commandant of Maoling, overseeing the area within its jurisdiction.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Tang was assigned to serve under the Prefect of the Capital, where he became a clerk under Ning Cheng. Ning Cheng, considering him a man of ability, recommended him to the central government. He was transferred to serve as Commandant of Maoling, where he oversaw the affairs of the district.

sentences.zh: 武安侯為丞相，徵湯為史，時薦言之天子，補御史，使案事。

translations.literal: When the Marquis of Wu'an became Chancellor, he summoned Tang to serve as his clerk, and from time to time recommended him to the Son of Heaven. Tang was appointed as a Censor and tasked with investigating cases.
translations.idiomatic: When the Marquis of Wu'an became Chancellor, he summoned Zhang Tang to serve on his staff and frequently recommended him to the emperor. Zhang Tang was appointed Censor and assigned to investigate cases.

sentences.zh: 治陳皇后蠱獄，深竟黨與。

translations.literal: He handled the witchcraft case of Empress Chen, thoroughly investigating and pursuing all those involved in the conspiracy.
translations.idiomatic: He took charge of the witchcraft case against Empress Chen, relentlessly pursuing every last member of the conspiracy.

sentences.zh: 於是上以為能，稍遷至太中大夫。

translations.literal: The emperor therefore considered him capable and gradually promoted him to Grand Palace Grandee.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor was impressed by his ability and gradually promoted him to the rank of Grand Palace Grandee.

sentences.zh: 與趙禹共定諸律令，務在深文，拘守職之吏。

translations.literal: Together with Zhao Yu he codified the various laws and edicts, striving for severe interpretations that constrained officials who adhered to their duties.
translations.idiomatic: Together with Zhao Yu, he codified the laws and edicts, striving above all for the harshest possible interpretations, which constrained even conscientious officials to the letter of the law.

sentences.zh: 已而趙禹遷為中尉，徙為少府，而張湯為廷尉，兩人交驩，而兄事禹。

translations.literal: Subsequently, Zhao Yu was promoted to Commandant of Justice and then transferred to Privy Treasurer, while Zhang Tang became Commandant of Trials; the two were close friends, and Tang treated Yu as an elder brother.
translations.idiomatic: In time, Zhao Yu was promoted to Commandant of Justice and then transferred to the post of Privy Treasurer, while Zhang Tang became Commandant of Trials. The two remained close friends, and Zhang Tang treated Zhao Yu as an elder brother.

sentences.zh: 禹為人廉倨。

translations.literal: Yu was incorruptible and proud by nature.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Yu was by nature incorruptible and haughty.

sentences.zh: 為吏以來，舍毋食客。

translations.literal: Since becoming an official, his household had no guests to feed.
translations.idiomatic: From the time he entered office, he kept no guests in his household.

sentences.zh: 公卿相造請禹，禹終不報謝，務在絕知友賓客之請，孤立行一意而已。

translations.literal: Ministers and nobles visited and invited Yu, yet Yu never returned their calls; he was determined to refuse all requests from friends and guests, standing alone and carrying out his single purpose.
translations.idiomatic: Nobles and ministers called upon Zhao Yu, yet he never returned their visits. He was determined to refuse every request from friends and associates, standing alone and pursuing his single-minded purpose.

sentences.zh: 見文法輒取，亦不覆案，求官屬陰罪。

translations.literal: Whenever he found a legal pretext, he would seize upon it, never reviewing the case again, and would seek out the hidden offenses of his subordinates.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever he found a legal pretext, he seized upon it without bothering to review the case further, and he would hunt out the hidden offenses of his subordinate officials.

sentences.zh: 湯為人多詐，舞智以御人。

translations.literal: Tang was a deceitful person, wielding his cleverness to manipulate people.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Tang, by contrast, was a man of great cunning who wielded his intelligence to manipulate others.

sentences.zh: 始為小吏，乾沒，與長安富賈田甲、魚翁叔之屬交私。

translations.literal: When he first served as a minor clerk, he speculated for profit and privately associated with wealthy Chang'an merchants such as Tian Jia and Yu Wengshu.
translations.idiomatic: In his early days as a minor clerk, he speculated for profit and cultivated private ties with wealthy Chang'an merchants like Tian Jia and Yu Wengshu.

sentences.zh: 及列九卿，收接天下名士大夫，己心內雖不合，然陽浮慕之。

translations.literal: When he rose to the rank of one of the Nine Ministers, he cultivated the renowned scholars and gentlemen of the realm; even if he inwardly disagreed with them, he outwardly affected admiration.
translations.idiomatic: After rising to the rank of one of the Nine Ministers, he cultivated the most renowned scholars and gentlemen throughout the realm. Even when he inwardly disagreed with them, he outwardly feigned admiration.

sentences.zh: 是時上方鄉文學，湯決大獄，欲傅古義，乃請博士弟子治尚書、春秋補廷尉史，亭疑法。

translations.literal: At that time the emperor was turning toward Confucian learning; when Tang judged major cases, he wished to support them with classical precedents, so he requested that Academician disciples versed in the Documents and the Spring and Autumn Annals be appointed as assistants in the Commandant of Trials' office to resolve doubtful points of law.
translations.idiomatic: At that time, the emperor was turning toward Confucian learning. When Zhang Tang adjudicated major cases, he wished to buttress his judgments with classical precedent. He therefore requested that disciples of the Imperial Academy versed in the Documents and the Spring and Autumn Annals be appointed to the Commandant of Trials' office to help resolve doubtful points of law.

sentences.zh: 奏讞疑事，必豫先為上分別其原，上所是，受而著讞決法廷尉絜令，揚主之明。

translations.literal: When presenting doubtful cases for judgment, he would always first lay out the origins of the case for the emperor in advance; whatever the emperor approved, he would accept and record as the official ruling of the Commandant of Trials, thus highlighting the emperor's sagacity.
translations.idiomatic: When presenting doubtful cases for the imperial judgment, he always laid out the merits of the case for the emperor beforehand. Whatever the emperor approved, he would adopt and record as the Commandant of Trials' official ruling, thereby magnifying the emperor's sagacity.

sentences.zh: 奏事即譴，湯應謝，鄉上意所便，必引正、監、掾史賢者，曰：「固為臣議，如上責臣，臣弗用，愚抵於此。」

translations.literal: If a memorial drew censure, Tang would apologize and follow the emperor's preference; he would invariably cite the worthy clerks, supervisors, and assistant officials, saying: 'They originally proposed this to your servant, but if Your Majesty censures your servant, it is because your servant did not adopt their advice, and stupidly came to this.'
translations.idiomatic: If a memorial drew the emperor's censure, Zhang Tang would at once apologize and defer to the imperial preference. He would invariably name his best clerks and supervisors, saying: "These men originally proposed this to me, but I failed to adopt their advice, and foolishly arrived at this result."

sentences.zh: 罪常釋。

translations.literal: His offenses were always pardoned.
translations.idiomatic: In this way, his offenses were invariably forgiven.

sentences.zh: （聞）［閒］即奏事，上善之，曰：「臣非知為此奏，乃正、監、掾史某為之。」

translations.literal: At other times, when he submitted a memorial and the emperor approved of it, he would say: 'Your servant did not know how to compose this memorial; it was actually the clerk so-and-so or supervisor so-and-so who wrote it.'
translations.idiomatic: Conversely, when he submitted a memorial the emperor approved of, he would say: "I myself did not know how to compose this memorial. It was actually prepared by such-and-such a clerk or supervisor."

sentences.zh: 其欲薦吏，揚人之善蔽人之過如此。

translations.literal: In his desire to recommend his officials, he promoted others' merits and concealed their faults in this way.
translations.idiomatic: Such was his method of recommending his subordinates — publicizing their merits and concealing their faults.

sentences.zh: 所治即上意所欲罪，予監史深禍者；

translations.literal: When the case was one the emperor wished to punish, he would assign it to supervisors and clerks who were harshest in sentencing;
translations.idiomatic: When a case was one the emperor wished to see punished, he would assign it to the most ruthless of his supervisors and clerks;

sentences.zh: 即上意所欲釋，與監史輕平者。

translations.literal: If the emperor wished to acquit, he would assign it to supervisors and clerks who were lenient and fair.
translations.idiomatic: when the emperor wished to see a case dismissed, he would assign it to those who were lenient and fair-minded.

sentences.zh: 所治即豪，必舞文巧詆；

translations.literal: When prosecuting powerful people, he would invariably twist the law to craft clever accusations;
translations.idiomatic: When prosecuting the powerful, he invariably manipulated the letter of the law to craft ingenious accusations;

sentences.zh: 即下戶羸弱，時口言，雖文致法，上財察。

translations.literal: When prosecuting the poor and weak, he would sometimes speak on their behalf; even though the law could technically be applied, the emperor would just barely scrutinize the case.
translations.idiomatic: When the accused were lowly and powerless, he would occasionally speak up for them. Even when the law could technically be invoked, the emperor would merely glance over the case.

sentences.zh: 於是往往釋湯所言。

translations.literal: Thus, the emperor would often accept what Tang said.
translations.idiomatic: And so the emperor would often accept Zhang Tang's recommendations.

sentences.zh: 湯至於大吏，內行修也。

translations.literal: By the time Tang became a high official, his private conduct was cultivated.
translations.idiomatic: By the time Zhang Tang had risen to high office, his private conduct appeared beyond reproach.

sentences.zh: 通賓客飲食。

translations.literal: He was generous in entertaining guests with food and drink.
translations.idiomatic: He was lavish in entertaining guests with food and wine.

sentences.zh: 於故人子弟為吏及貧昆弟，調護之尤厚。

translations.literal: Toward the sons and younger brothers of old friends who served as officials, and toward poor kinsmen, he was especially generous in looking after them.
translations.idiomatic: He was especially generous in looking after the sons of old friends who served in office, as well as his own impoverished kinsmen.

sentences.zh: 其造請諸公，不避寒暑。

translations.literal: In visiting and calling upon the great officials, he shunned neither cold nor heat.
translations.idiomatic: When calling upon the great ministers, he braved both the cold of winter and the heat of summer.

sentences.zh: 是以湯雖文深意忌不專平，然得此聲譽。

translations.literal: For this reason, although Tang's legal interpretations were harsh, his temperament jealous, and his judgments not always fair, he still acquired this reputation.
translations.idiomatic: For this reason, although Zhang Tang's legal interpretations were severe, his temperament jealous, and his judgments far from impartial, he nonetheless earned a fine reputation.

sentences.zh: 而刻深吏多為爪牙用者，依於文學之士。

translations.literal: And the harsh officials who served as his trusted agents would attach themselves to Confucian scholars.
translations.idiomatic: The harsh officials who served as his trusted agents attached themselves to Confucian scholars for cover.

sentences.zh: 丞相弘數稱其美。

translations.literal: Chancellor Hong frequently praised his virtues.
translations.idiomatic: Chancellor Gongsun Hong frequently praised his merits.

sentences.zh: 及治淮南、衡山、江都反獄，皆窮根本。

translations.literal: When he handled the rebellion cases of Huainan, Hengshan, and Jiangdu, he pursued every case to its root.
translations.idiomatic: When he handled the rebellion cases of Huainan, Hengshan, and Jiangdu, he pursued every last root of the conspiracies.

sentences.zh: 嚴助及伍被，上欲釋之。

translations.literal: Regarding Yan Zhu and Wu Bei, the emperor wished to pardon them.
translations.idiomatic: In the cases of Yan Zhu and Wu Bei, the emperor wished to pardon them.

sentences.zh: 湯爭曰：「伍被本畫反謀，而助親幸出入禁闥爪牙臣，乃交私諸侯如此，弗誅，後不可治。」

translations.literal: Tang argued: 'Wu Bei was the original architect of the rebellion plot, and Yan Zhu, a favored intimate with access to the imperial chambers and a trusted minister, secretly consorted with the feudal lords in this manner. If they are not executed, governance will be impossible in the future.'
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Tang argued strenuously: "Wu Bei was the very architect of the rebellion plot, and Yan Zhu, a trusted intimate with access to the inner court, secretly consorted with the feudal lords. If they are not executed, there will be no governing the realm in the future."

sentences.zh: 於是上可論之。

translations.literal: Thereupon the emperor approved their prosecution.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor thereupon consented to their prosecution.

sentences.zh: 其治獄所排大臣自為功，多此類。

translations.literal: In his handling of legal cases, he often pushed aside great ministers and claimed the credit for himself; many cases were of this kind.
translations.idiomatic: In his handling of cases, he frequently brought down great ministers while claiming the credit himself. Many of his cases followed this pattern.

sentences.zh: 於是湯益尊任，遷為御史大夫。

translations.literal: Thus Tang was increasingly honored and entrusted, and was promoted to Imperial Secretary.
translations.idiomatic: As a result, Zhang Tang's prestige and authority grew, and he was promoted to the rank of Imperial Secretary.

sentences.zh: 會渾邪等降，漢大興兵伐匈奴，山東水旱，貧民流徙，皆仰給縣官，縣官空虛。

translations.literal: It happened that Hunye and others surrendered, the Han raised great armies to attack the Xiongnu, floods and droughts struck east of the mountains, impoverished people became refugees, all depending on the government for support, and the government coffers were emptied.
translations.idiomatic: It happened that Hunye and other Xiongnu leaders surrendered, and the Han raised vast armies to campaign against the Xiongnu. Meanwhile, floods and droughts struck the lands east of the mountains, impoverished people became refugees, all dependent on the government for relief, and the state coffers were drained dry.

sentences.zh: 於是丞上指，請造白金及五銖錢，籠天下鹽鐵，排富商大賈，出告緡令，鉏豪彊并兼之家，舞文巧詆以輔法。

translations.literal: Thereupon, following the emperor's intentions, he requested the minting of white-metal coins and five-zhu coins, monopolized the salt and iron of the realm, suppressed wealthy merchants and great traders, issued the decree on reporting concealed wealth, rooted out the powerful families who had been engrossing property, and manipulated legal texts with clever accusations to enforce the law.
translations.idiomatic: Accordingly, acting on the emperor's wishes, Zhang Tang proposed the minting of white-metal coins and five-zhu coins, established state monopolies over salt and iron, suppressed the great merchants and traders, promulgated the decree requiring the reporting of concealed wealth, uprooted the powerful families who had been amassing estates, and twisted the letter of the law with ingenious accusations to enforce compliance.

sentences.zh: 湯每朝奏事，語國家用，日晏，天子忘食。

translations.literal: Whenever Tang attended court to present matters and discussed state finances, the day grew late and the Son of Heaven forgot to eat.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever Zhang Tang attended court to present his proposals and discuss state finances, the discussions would stretch until late in the day, and the Son of Heaven would forget to eat.

sentences.zh: 丞相取充位，天下事皆決於湯。

translations.literal: The Chancellor merely occupied his position, and all affairs of the realm were decided by Tang.
translations.idiomatic: The Chancellor was reduced to a mere figurehead, and all the affairs of the realm were decided by Zhang Tang.

sentences.zh: 百姓不安其生，騷動，縣官所興，未獲其利，姦吏并侵漁，於是痛繩以罪。

translations.literal: The common people could not live in peace, they were in upheaval; the government's new enterprises had not yet yielded their benefits, and corrupt officials exploited and plundered the people. Thereupon heavy penalties were imposed.
translations.idiomatic: The common people could find no peace and were in a state of upheaval. The government's new enterprises had not yet yielded any benefit, and corrupt officials exploited and plundered at every turn. In response, ever harsher punishments were imposed.

sentences.zh: 則自公卿以下，至於庶人，咸指湯。

translations.literal: Then from the ministers and nobles down to the common people, all pointed at Tang.
translations.idiomatic: From the highest ministers down to the humblest commoners, all pointed the finger at Zhang Tang.

sentences.zh: 湯嘗病，天子至自視病，其隆貴如此。

translations.literal: When Tang once fell ill, the Son of Heaven went in person to visit him in his sickness — such was the eminence of his favor.
translations.idiomatic: Once, when Zhang Tang fell ill, the Son of Heaven came in person to visit him at his sickbed — such was the height of his favor.

sentences.zh: 匈奴來請和親，群臣議上前。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu came to request a marriage alliance, and the assembled ministers debated before the emperor.
translations.idiomatic: When the Xiongnu sent envoys to request a marriage alliance, the assembled ministers debated the matter before the emperor.

sentences.zh: 博士狄山曰：「和親便。」

translations.literal: The Academician Di Shan said: 'A marriage alliance is the expedient course.'
translations.idiomatic: The Academician Di Shan declared: "A marriage alliance is the advantageous course."

sentences.zh: 上問其便，山曰：「兵者凶器，未易數動。

translations.literal: The emperor asked why it was advantageous, and Shan said: 'Weapons are instruments of ill omen, and should not be set in motion lightly or often.'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor asked why it was advantageous. Di Shan replied: "Weapons are instruments of ill omen, not to be set in motion lightly.

sentences.zh: 高帝欲伐匈奴，大困平城，乃遂結和親。

translations.literal: Emperor Gao wished to attack the Xiongnu but was greatly besieged at Pingcheng, and thereafter concluded a marriage alliance.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Gao set out to crush the Xiongnu but was besieged at Pingcheng. He thereupon concluded a marriage alliance.

sentences.zh: 孝惠、高后時，天下安樂。

translations.literal: During the reigns of Emperor Hui and Empress Dowager Gao, the realm was at peace and the people content.
translations.idiomatic: During the reigns of Emperor Hui and Empress Dowager Gao, the realm was at peace and the people content.

sentences.zh: 及孝文帝欲事匈奴，北邊蕭然苦兵矣。

translations.literal: When Emperor Wen wished to campaign against the Xiongnu, the northern frontier became desolate and suffered the hardships of war.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Wen turned his attention to the Xiongnu, the northern frontier was left desolate, ravaged by the hardships of war.

sentences.zh: 孝景時，吳楚七國反，景帝往來兩宮閒，寒心者數月。

translations.literal: In Emperor Jing's time, the seven kingdoms of Wu and Chu rebelled; Emperor Jing went back and forth between the two palaces, his heart chilled with fear for several months.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Jing, the seven kingdoms led by Wu and Chu rebelled. Emperor Jing paced between the two palaces, his heart gripped with dread for months on end.

sentences.zh: 吳楚已破，竟景帝不言兵，天下富實。

translations.literal: After Wu and Chu were crushed, Emperor Jing never again spoke of war for the rest of his reign, and the realm grew wealthy and prosperous.
translations.idiomatic: After Wu and Chu were crushed, Emperor Jing never again spoke of war for the remainder of his reign, and the realm grew wealthy and prosperous."

sentences.zh: 今自陛下舉兵擊匈奴，中國以空虛，邊民大困貧。

translations.literal: Now, since Your Majesty raised armies to strike the Xiongnu, the interior has been drained empty, and the border people are impoverished and in great distress.
translations.idiomatic: Now, since Your Majesty raised armies against the Xiongnu, the interior of the empire has been drained dry, and the people of the frontier are impoverished and in dire distress.

sentences.zh: 由此觀之，不如和親。」

translations.literal: Viewed from this perspective, a marriage alliance is the better course.
translations.idiomatic: Viewed from this perspective, a marriage alliance is the better course."

sentences.zh: 上問湯，湯曰：「此愚儒，無知。」

translations.literal: The emperor asked Tang, and Tang said: 'This is a foolish Confucian, ignorant.'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor turned to Zhang Tang, who replied: "This is a foolish pedant who knows nothing."

sentences.zh: 狄山曰：「臣固愚忠，若御史大夫湯乃詐忠。

translations.literal: Di Shan said: 'Your servant is indeed foolishly loyal, but the Imperial Secretary Zhang Tang is falsely loyal.'
translations.idiomatic: Di Shan shot back: "I may be foolishly loyal, but the Imperial Secretary Zhang Tang is loyal only in pretense.

sentences.zh: 若湯之治淮南、江都，以深文痛詆諸侯，別疏骨肉，使蕃臣不自安。

translations.literal: In the way Tang handled the Huainan and Jiangdu cases, he used harsh legal interpretations to viciously denounce the feudal lords, alienated flesh and blood relatives, and made the vassal ministers feel insecure.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhang Tang handled the cases of Huainan and Jiangdu, he used the harshest legal interpretations to viciously denounce the feudal lords, drove apart flesh and blood, and left the vassal lords unable to sleep in peace.

sentences.zh: 臣固知湯之為詐忠。」

translations.literal: Your servant knows full well that Tang's loyalty is false.
translations.idiomatic: I know perfectly well that Zhang Tang's loyalty is nothing but pretense."

sentences.zh: 於是上作色曰：「吾使生居一郡，能無使虜入盜乎？」

translations.literal: At this the emperor's face darkened, and he said: 'If I were to station you in a commandery, could you prevent the barbarians from raiding?'
translations.idiomatic: At this the emperor's face darkened. "If I were to station you in a commandery," he said, "could you prevent the barbarians from raiding?"

sentences.zh: 曰：「不能。」

translations.literal: He said: 'I could not.'
translations.idiomatic: Di Shan replied: "I could not."

sentences.zh: 曰：「居一縣？」

translations.literal: The emperor said: 'What about a single county?'
translations.idiomatic: "What about a single county?"

sentences.zh: 對曰：「不能。」

translations.literal: He replied: 'I could not.'
translations.idiomatic: "I could not."

sentences.zh: 復曰：「居一障閒？」

translations.literal: The emperor again asked: 'What about a single border fortress?'
translations.idiomatic: "Then what about a single border fortress?"

sentences.zh: 山自度辯窮且下吏，曰：「能。」

translations.literal: Shan, realizing that his arguments were exhausted and he would be handed over to the law officials, said: 'I can.'
translations.idiomatic: Di Shan, sensing his arguments were exhausted and he would next be handed over to the judicial officials, replied: "That I can do."

sentences.zh: 於是上遣山乘鄣。

translations.literal: Thereupon the emperor sent Shan to man a border fortress.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor thereupon dispatched Di Shan to man a border fortress.

sentences.zh: 至月餘，匈奴斬山頭而去。

translations.literal: After a little more than a month, the Xiongnu cut off Shan's head and departed.
translations.idiomatic: After little more than a month, the Xiongnu cut off Di Shan's head and rode away.

sentences.zh: 自是以後，群臣震慴。

translations.literal: From this time on, the assembled ministers were struck with terror.
translations.idiomatic: From that time on, the assembled ministers were struck dumb with terror.

sentences.zh: 湯之客田甲，雖賈人，有賢操。

translations.literal: Tang's associate Tian Jia, although a merchant, possessed virtuous principles.
translations.idiomatic: Among Zhang Tang's associates was Tian Jia, who, though a merchant, was a man of upright character.

sentences.zh: 始湯為小吏時，與錢通，及湯為大吏，甲所以責湯行義過失，亦有烈士風。

translations.literal: When Tang was first a minor clerk, they had financial dealings; when Tang became a high official, Jia would reproach Tang for lapses in righteous conduct, displaying the spirit of a man of honor.
translations.idiomatic: In Zhang Tang's early days as a minor clerk, the two had financial dealings. After Zhang Tang rose to high office, Tian Jia would reproach him for lapses in moral conduct, displaying the spirit of a man of honor.

sentences.zh: 湯為御史大夫七歲，敗。

translations.literal: Tang served as Imperial Secretary for seven years before his downfall.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Tang served as Imperial Secretary for seven years before his downfall.

sentences.zh: 河東人李文嘗與湯有卻，已而為御史中丞，恚，數從中文書事有可以傷湯者，不能為地。

translations.literal: Li Wen of Hedong had once had a quarrel with Tang; later, when he became Vice Censor, resentful, he repeatedly seized upon documents and cases from the court that could damage Tang, giving Tang no room.
translations.idiomatic: Li Wen of Hedong had once had a quarrel with Zhang Tang. Later, after becoming Vice Censor, Li Wen nursed his grudge and repeatedly seized upon court documents and cases that could be used against Zhang Tang, leaving him no room to maneuver.

sentences.zh: 湯有所愛史魯謁居，知湯不平，使人上蜚變告文姦事，事下湯，湯治論殺文，而湯心知謁居為之。

translations.literal: Tang had a favored clerk named Lu Yeju, who knew of Tang's resentment, and had someone submit an anonymous accusation of Li Wen's criminal activities. The case was referred to Tang, and Tang tried and executed Li Wen, knowing in his heart that Yeju was behind it.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Tang had a favored clerk named Lu Yeju who was aware of his resentment. Lu Yeju had someone submit an anonymous accusation of Li Wen's criminal activities. The case was referred to Zhang Tang, who tried and executed Li Wen — knowing perfectly well that Lu Yeju had engineered the whole affair.

sentences.zh: 上問曰：「言變事縱跡安起？」

translations.literal: The emperor asked: 'From where did this accusation and its evidence originate?'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor asked: "Where did this accusation and its evidence originate?"

sentences.zh: 湯詳驚曰：「此殆文故人怨之。」

translations.literal: Tang feigned surprise and said: 'This must have been old acquaintances of Li Wen bearing a grudge against him.'
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Tang feigned surprise and replied: "This must have been the work of old acquaintances of Li Wen who bore a grudge against him."

sentences.zh: 謁居病臥閭里主人，湯自往視疾，為謁居摩足。

translations.literal: When Yeju fell ill and lay bedridden at his host's home in the neighborhood, Tang personally went to visit him and massaged Yeju's feet.
translations.idiomatic: When Lu Yeju fell ill and lay bedridden at a neighbor's house, Zhang Tang personally went to visit him and even massaged his feet.

sentences.zh: 趙國以冶鑄為業，王數訟鐵官事，湯常排趙王。

translations.literal: The kingdom of Zhao relied on smelting and casting as its livelihood; the king frequently brought suit over iron-office matters, and Tang consistently ruled against the King of Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: The kingdom of Zhao relied on smelting and casting as its livelihood. The king frequently brought suit over matters involving the iron offices, and Zhang Tang consistently ruled against him.

sentences.zh: 趙王求湯陰事。

translations.literal: The King of Zhao sought to discover Tang's secret misdeeds.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Zhao set about uncovering Zhang Tang's secret misdeeds.

sentences.zh: 謁居嘗案趙王，趙王怨之，并上書告：「湯，大臣也，史謁居有病，湯至為摩足，疑與為大姦。」

translations.literal: Yeju had once investigated the King of Zhao, and the king bore a grudge; he submitted a memorial stating: 'Tang is a great minister, yet when his clerk Yeju fell ill, Tang went so far as to massage his feet. I suspect they are conspiring in some great crime.'
translations.idiomatic: Lu Yeju had once investigated the King of Zhao, and the king bore him a grudge. He submitted a memorial: "Zhang Tang is a great minister, yet when his clerk Lu Yeju fell ill, Zhang Tang went so far as to massage his feet. I suspect they are conspiring together in some grave crime."

sentences.zh: 事下廷尉。

translations.literal: The case was referred to the Commandant of Trials.
translations.idiomatic: The case was referred to the Commandant of Trials.

sentences.zh: 謁居病死，事連其弟，弟系導官。

translations.literal: Yeju died of his illness; the case implicated his younger brother, who was imprisoned in the grain-processing office.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Yeju died of his illness, but the case implicated his younger brother, who was arrested and held in the grain-processing office.

sentences.zh: 湯亦治他囚導官，見謁居弟，欲陰為之，而詳不省。

translations.literal: Tang was also handling other prisoners at the grain-processing office; he saw Yeju's brother and wished to secretly help him, yet pretended not to recognize him.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Tang happened to be handling other prisoners at the grain-processing office. He saw Lu Yeju's brother and wished to secretly assist him, but pretended not to know who he was.

sentences.zh: 謁居弟弗知，怨湯，使人上書告湯與謁居謀，共變告李文。

translations.literal: Yeju's brother did not know this, resented Tang, and had someone submit a memorial accusing Tang of conspiring with Yeju to fabricate the accusation against Li Wen.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Yeju's brother, unaware of Zhang Tang's intentions, bore a grudge against him and had someone submit a memorial accusing Zhang Tang of conspiring with Lu Yeju to fabricate the case against Li Wen.

sentences.zh: 事下減宣。

translations.literal: The case was referred to Jian Xuan.
translations.idiomatic: The case was referred to Jian Xuan for investigation.

sentences.zh: 宣嘗與湯有卻，及得此事，窮竟其事，未奏也。

translations.literal: Xuan had once had a quarrel with Tang; upon receiving this case, he investigated it thoroughly to its end, but had not yet submitted a report.
translations.idiomatic: Jian Xuan had once had a quarrel with Zhang Tang. Upon receiving this case, he pursued the investigation to its fullest, though he had not yet submitted his report.

sentences.zh: 會人有盜發孝文園瘞錢，丞相青翟朝，與湯約俱謝，至前，湯念獨丞相以四時行園，當謝，湯無與也，不謝。

translations.literal: It happened that someone had stolen the burial money from Emperor Wen's funerary park. Chancellor Qingdi, at court, agreed with Tang that they would apologize together. When they came before the emperor, Tang reflected that only the Chancellor was responsible for the seasonal inspections of the park and should apologize, and that he himself had no involvement, so he did not apologize.
translations.idiomatic: It happened that someone had stolen the burial money from Emperor Wen's funerary park. At court, Chancellor Qingdi had agreed with Zhang Tang that they would both take responsibility. But when the moment came, Zhang Tang reflected that only the Chancellor was responsible for the seasonal inspections of the park, and that he himself had no part in it. So he refused to apologize.

sentences.zh: 丞相謝，上使御史案其事。

translations.literal: The Chancellor apologized, and the emperor ordered the Censors to investigate the matter.
translations.idiomatic: The Chancellor apologized alone, and the emperor ordered the Censors to investigate.

sentences.zh: 湯欲致其文丞相見知，丞相患之。

translations.literal: Tang wished to use legal text to implicate the Chancellor under the 'obligation to report' statute; the Chancellor was greatly troubled by this.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Tang sought to use the "obligation to report" statute to implicate the Chancellor. The Chancellor was deeply alarmed.

sentences.zh: 三長史皆害湯，欲陷之。

translations.literal: The three Senior Clerks all resented Tang and wished to bring about his downfall.
translations.idiomatic: The three Senior Clerks all bore grudges against Zhang Tang and wished to bring about his downfall.

sentences.zh: 始長史朱買臣，會稽人也。

translations.literal: The first Senior Clerk was Zhu Maichen, a native of Kuaiji.
translations.idiomatic: The first of the Senior Clerks was Zhu Maichen, a native of Kuaiji.

sentences.zh: 讀春秋。

translations.literal: He studied the Spring and Autumn Annals.
translations.idiomatic: He was a student of the Spring and Autumn Annals.

sentences.zh: 莊助使人言買臣，買臣以楚辭與助俱幸，侍中，為太中大夫，用事；

translations.literal: Zhuang Zhu had someone recommend Maichen; Maichen, on account of his skill with the Songs of Chu, rose in favor alongside Zhuang Zhu, serving as Attendant within the Palace and then Grand Palace Grandee, wielding power;
translations.idiomatic: Zhuang Zhu had someone recommend Zhu Maichen to the emperor. On the strength of his mastery of the Songs of Chu, Zhu Maichen rose in favor alongside Zhuang Zhu, serving as an Attendant within the Palace and then as Grand Palace Grandee, wielding considerable influence.

sentences.zh: 而湯乃為小吏，跪伏使買臣等前。

translations.literal: At that time, Tang was merely a minor clerk, kneeling and prostrating himself to let Maichen and others pass before him.
translations.idiomatic: In those days, Zhang Tang was still a lowly clerk, kneeling and prostrating himself to let Zhu Maichen and his like pass before him.

sentences.zh: 已而湯為廷尉，治淮南獄，排擠莊助，買臣固心望。

translations.literal: Subsequently, when Tang became Commandant of Trials and handled the Huainan case, he pushed aside Zhuang Zhu, and Maichen had long harbored resentment.
translations.idiomatic: Later, when Zhang Tang became Commandant of Trials and took charge of the Huainan case, he brought about the downfall of Zhuang Zhu. Zhu Maichen, who had long nursed his resentment, was aggrieved.

sentences.zh: 及湯為御史大夫，買臣以會稽守為主爵都尉，列於九卿。

translations.literal: When Tang became Imperial Secretary, Maichen had gone from being Governor of Kuaiji to serving as Commandant of Bestowing Nobility, ranking among the Nine Ministers.
translations.idiomatic: By the time Zhang Tang became Imperial Secretary, Zhu Maichen had advanced from being Governor of Kuaiji to serving as Commandant of Bestowing Nobility, ranked among the Nine Ministers.

sentences.zh: 數年，坐法廢，守長史，見湯，湯坐床上，丞史遇買臣弗為禮。

translations.literal: After several years, he was dismissed for a legal offense and served as Senior Clerk. When he called on Tang, Tang sat on his couch while his clerks treated Maichen without courtesy.
translations.idiomatic: After several years, Zhu Maichen was dismissed for a legal offense and reduced to serving as a Senior Clerk. When he called upon Zhang Tang, Tang remained seated on his couch, and his assistant clerks treated Zhu Maichen without the least courtesy.

sentences.zh: 買臣楚士，深怨，常欲死之。

translations.literal: Maichen, a proud man of Chu, harbored a deep grudge and constantly wished to destroy him.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Maichen, a proud man of Chu, nursed a bitter grudge and constantly wished for Zhang Tang's destruction.

sentences.zh: 王朝，齊人也。

translations.literal: Wang Chao was a man from Qi.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Chao was a native of Qi.

sentences.zh: 以術至右內史。

translations.literal: Through political skill he rose to the rank of Right Prefect of the Capital.
translations.idiomatic: Through political maneuvering, he had risen to the rank of Right Prefect of the Capital.

sentences.zh: 邊通，學長短，剛暴彊人也，官再至濟南相。

translations.literal: Bian Tong, who studied the arts of strategic persuasion, was a forceful, violent, and headstrong man; he had twice served as Chancellor of Jinan.
translations.idiomatic: Bian Tong, a student of the arts of strategic persuasion, was a forceful, violent, and headstrong man who had twice served as Chancellor of Jinan.

sentences.zh: 故皆居湯右，已而失官，守長史，詘體於湯。

translations.literal: All of them had formerly ranked above Tang; but after losing their positions, they served as Senior Clerks and had to humble themselves before Tang.
translations.idiomatic: All three had formerly ranked above Zhang Tang, but after losing their positions, they were reduced to serving as Senior Clerks and had to humble themselves before him.

sentences.zh: 湯數行丞相事，知此三長史素貴，常淩折之。

translations.literal: Tang frequently acted in the Chancellor's stead and, knowing that these three Senior Clerks had once been men of rank, constantly humiliated and demeaned them.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Tang frequently acted in the Chancellor's stead and, knowing that these three men had once held distinguished rank, constantly humiliated and demeaned them.

sentences.zh: 以故三長史合謀曰：「始湯約與君謝，已而賣君；

translations.literal: For this reason, the three Senior Clerks conspired together and said: 'At first Tang agreed with our lord the Chancellor to apologize, but then he betrayed him;'
translations.idiomatic: The three Senior Clerks therefore conspired together and said to the Chancellor: "At first Zhang Tang agreed to apologize alongside you, but then he betrayed you.

sentences.zh: 今欲劾君以宗廟事，此欲代君耳。

translations.literal: Now he wishes to impeach our lord over the ancestral temple affair; this is merely to replace him.
translations.idiomatic: Now he means to impeach you over the ancestral temple affair — he simply wants to take your place.

sentences.zh: 吾知湯陰事。」

translations.literal: We know Tang's secret misdeeds.
translations.idiomatic: We know Zhang Tang's secret misdeeds."

sentences.zh: 使吏捕案湯左田信等，曰湯且欲奏請，信輒先知之，居物致富，與湯分之，及他姦事。

translations.literal: They sent officials to arrest and investigate Tang's associate Tian Xin and others, saying that whenever Tang was about to submit a memorial, Xin would learn of it in advance, hoard goods to grow wealthy, and share the profits with Tang, along with other criminal activities.
translations.idiomatic: They dispatched officials to arrest and investigate Zhang Tang's associate Tian Xin and others, alleging that whenever Zhang Tang was about to submit a policy memorial, Tian Xin would learn of it in advance, corner the relevant goods to enrich himself, and split the profits with Zhang Tang — along with other criminal dealings.

sentences.zh: 事辭頗聞。

translations.literal: The details of the case became somewhat widely known.
translations.idiomatic: The details of the case became widely known.

sentences.zh: 上問湯曰：「吾所為，賈人輒先知之，益居其物，是類有以吾謀告之者。」

translations.literal: The emperor asked Tang: 'Whatever policy I adopt, the merchants always learn of it beforehand and hoard the relevant goods. It seems as though someone is leaking my plans to them.'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor confronted Zhang Tang: "Whatever policy I adopt, the merchants always learn of it in advance and corner the relevant goods. It appears that someone is leaking my plans to them."

sentences.zh: 湯不謝。

translations.literal: Tang did not apologize.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Tang offered no apology.

sentences.zh: 湯又詳驚曰：「固宜有。」

translations.literal: Tang again feigned surprise and said: 'There must surely be such a person.'
translations.idiomatic: Instead, he feigned surprise and said: "There must surely be such a person."

sentences.zh: 減宣亦奏謁居等事。

translations.literal: Jian Xuan also submitted a report on the affair of Yeju and others.
translations.idiomatic: At the same time, Jian Xuan submitted his report on the Lu Yeju affair.

sentences.zh: 天子果以湯懷詐面欺，使使八輩簿責湯。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven indeed concluded that Tang harbored deceit and had deceived him to his face, and sent eight successive envoys to interrogate Tang with documentary evidence.
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven concluded that Zhang Tang had indeed harbored deceit and lied to his face. He dispatched eight successive envoys to interrogate Zhang Tang with documentary evidence.

sentences.zh: 湯具自道無此，不服。

translations.literal: Tang gave a full account denying these charges and refused to submit.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Tang gave a detailed denial of the charges and refused to confess.

sentences.zh: 於是上使趙禹責湯。

translations.literal: Thereupon the emperor sent Zhao Yu to interrogate Tang.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor thereupon sent Zhao Yu to confront Zhang Tang.

sentences.zh: 禹至，讓湯曰：「君何不知分也。

translations.literal: When Yu arrived, he rebuked Tang, saying: 'Why do you not know your place?'
translations.idiomatic: When Zhao Yu arrived, he rebuked Zhang Tang: "Why do you not understand your situation?

sentences.zh: 君所治夷滅者幾何人矣？

translations.literal: How many people have you investigated and destroyed?
translations.idiomatic: How many men have you investigated and destroyed?

sentences.zh: 今人言君皆有狀，天子重致君獄，欲令君自為計，何多以對簿為？」

translations.literal: Now the accusations against you are all substantiated. The Son of Heaven finds it hard to bring you to trial and wishes you to settle the matter yourself. Why insist on answering charges at a formal hearing?
translations.idiomatic: Now the accusations against you are all substantiated. The Son of Heaven finds it difficult to subject you to a trial and wishes you to settle the matter yourself. Why insist on dragging this out at a formal hearing?"

sentences.zh: 湯乃為書謝曰：「湯無尺寸功，起刀筆吏，陛下幸致為三公，無以塞責。

translations.literal: Tang then wrote a letter of apology saying: 'Tang has no inch of merit; rising from a minor clerk, Your Majesty graciously raised me to the Three Excellencies. I have nothing with which to discharge my blame.'
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Tang then wrote a letter of final apology: "I, Zhang Tang, have not an inch of merit to my name. Rising from a petty clerk, I was graced by Your Majesty with elevation to the Three Excellencies. I have nothing with which to answer for my failings.

sentences.zh: 然謀陷湯罪者，三長史也。」

translations.literal: However, those who plotted to entrap Tang in this crime are the three Senior Clerks.
translations.idiomatic: Yet those who conspired to entrap me are the three Senior Clerks."

sentences.zh: 遂自殺。

translations.literal: He then killed himself.
translations.idiomatic: And with that, he took his own life.

sentences.zh: 湯死，家產直不過五百金，皆所得奉賜，無他業。

translations.literal: When Tang died, his family property was worth no more than five hundred catties of gold, all from official salary and imperial gifts, with no other enterprises.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhang Tang died, his family's entire estate was worth no more than five hundred catties of gold — all of it from his official salary and imperial gifts, with no other source of income.

sentences.zh: 昆弟諸子欲厚葬湯，湯母曰：「湯為天子大臣，被汙惡言而死，何厚葬乎！」

translations.literal: His brothers and sons wished to give Tang a lavish burial, but Tang's mother said: 'Tang was a great minister of the Son of Heaven, yet died under the stain of vile accusations — what call is there for a lavish burial!'
translations.idiomatic: His brothers and sons wished to give him a lavish burial, but Zhang Tang's mother said: "Zhang Tang was a great minister of the Son of Heaven, yet he died under the stain of vile accusations. What need is there for a lavish burial?"

sentences.zh: 載以牛車，有棺無槨。

translations.literal: He was carried on an ox-cart, with an inner coffin but no outer coffin.
translations.idiomatic: He was carried to his grave on an ox-cart, with an inner coffin but no outer.

sentences.zh: 天子聞之，曰：「非此母不能生此子。」

translations.literal: When the Son of Heaven heard this, he said: 'Only such a mother could have borne such a son.'
translations.idiomatic: When the Son of Heaven heard of this, he remarked: "Only such a mother could have borne such a son."

sentences.zh: 乃盡案誅三長史。

translations.literal: He then investigated and executed all three Senior Clerks.
translations.idiomatic: He then had all three Senior Clerks investigated and executed.

sentences.zh: 丞相青翟自殺。

translations.literal: Chancellor Qingdi committed suicide.
translations.idiomatic: Chancellor Qingdi took his own life.

sentences.zh: 出田信。

translations.literal: Tian Xin was released.
translations.idiomatic: Tian Xin was released from custody.

sentences.zh: 上惜湯。

translations.literal: The emperor mourned Tang.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor mourned Zhang Tang's loss.

sentences.zh: 稍遷其子安世。

translations.literal: He gradually promoted Tang's son Anshi.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor gradually promoted Zhang Tang's son, Zhang Anshi.

sentences.zh: 趙禹中廢，已而為廷尉。

translations.literal: Zhao Yu was dismissed from office for a time, but subsequently became Commandant of Trials.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Yu fell from office for a time but was subsequently appointed Commandant of Trials.

sentences.zh: 始條侯以為禹賊深，弗任。

translations.literal: Previously, the Marquis of Tiao had considered Yu dangerously severe and refused to promote him.
translations.idiomatic: Previously, the Marquis of Tiao had judged Zhao Yu dangerously severe and had refused to promote him.

sentences.zh: 及禹為少府，比九卿。

translations.literal: When Yu became Privy Treasurer, he ranked among the Nine Ministers.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhao Yu became Privy Treasurer, he at last ranked among the Nine Ministers.

sentences.zh: 禹酷急，至晚節，事益多，吏務為嚴峻，而禹治加緩，而名為平。

translations.literal: Yu had been harsh and exacting; but in his later years, as affairs multiplied and officials strove to be ever more severe, Yu's governance became comparatively lenient, and he gained a reputation for fairness.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Yu had always been harsh and exacting. But in his later years, as affairs multiplied and officials competed in severity, his own governance grew comparatively lenient, and he came to be known for his fairness.

sentences.zh: 王溫舒等后起，治酷於禹。

translations.literal: Wang Wenshu and others rose after him and governed more harshly than Yu.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Wenshu and others who came after him governed with a cruelty that far exceeded Zhao Yu's.

sentences.zh: 禹以老，徙為燕相。

translations.literal: Yu, on account of his age, was transferred to serve as Chancellor of Yan.
translations.idiomatic: In his old age, Zhao Yu was transferred to the post of Chancellor of Yan.

sentences.zh: 數歲，亂悖有罪，免歸。

translations.literal: After several years, he was found guilty of misconduct and dismissed, returning home.
translations.idiomatic: After several years, he was found guilty of misconduct, dismissed, and sent home.

sentences.zh: 後湯十餘年，以壽卒于家。

translations.literal: More than ten years after Tang, he died of old age at home.
translations.idiomatic: More than ten years after Zhang Tang's death, Zhao Yu died of old age at home.

sentences.zh: 義縱者，河東人也。

translations.literal: Yi Zong was a man from Hedong.
translations.idiomatic: Yi Zong was a native of Hedong.

sentences.zh: 為少年時，嘗與張次公俱攻剽為群盜。

translations.literal: In his youth, he once joined Zhang Cigong in robbing and plundering as part of a bandit gang.
translations.idiomatic: In his youth, he had joined Zhang Cigong in raiding and plundering as part of a bandit gang.

sentences.zh: 縱有姊姁，以醫幸王太后。

translations.literal: Zong had an elder sister named Xu, who won the favor of Empress Dowager Wang through her medical skill.
translations.idiomatic: Yi Zong had an elder sister named Xu who won the favor of Empress Dowager Wang through her skill as a physician.

sentences.zh: 王太后問：「有子兄弟為官者乎？」

translations.literal: Empress Dowager Wang asked: 'Do you have any sons or brothers who serve in office?'
translations.idiomatic: Empress Dowager Wang asked her: "Do you have any sons or brothers serving in office?"

sentences.zh: 姊曰：「有弟無行，不可。」

translations.literal: The sister said: 'I have a younger brother, but he is of bad character and would not do.'
translations.idiomatic: Her sister replied: "I have a younger brother, but he is of bad character. He would not be suitable."

sentences.zh: 太后乃告上，拜義姁弟縱為中郎，補上黨郡中令。

translations.literal: The Empress Dowager then told the emperor, who appointed Xu's younger brother Zong as a Gentleman of the Palace and assigned him as County Magistrate in Shangdang Commandery.
