translations.idiomatic: The Empress Dowager spoke to the emperor, who appointed Xu's younger brother, Yi Zong, as a Gentleman of the Palace and assigned him as a county magistrate in Shangdang Commandery.

sentences.zh: 治敢行，少蘊藉，縣無逋事，舉為第一。

translations.literal: His governance was bold and decisive, with little leniency; the county had no backlog of cases, and he was rated first.
translations.idiomatic: His governance was bold and decisive, with little room for leniency. The county had no backlog of cases, and he was rated first among his peers.

sentences.zh: 遷為長陵及長安令，直法行治，不避貴戚。

translations.literal: He was promoted to Magistrate of Changling and then of Chang'an, enforcing the law straightforwardly and not sparing the noble or the wellborn.
translations.idiomatic: He was promoted to Magistrate of Changling and then of Chang'an, enforcing the law without compromise and sparing neither the noble nor the wellborn.

sentences.zh: 以捕案太后外孫修成君子仲，上以為能，遷為河內都尉。

translations.literal: Because he arrested and prosecuted the Empress Dowager's grandson through her daughter, Lord Xiucheng's son Zhong, the emperor considered him capable and promoted him to Commandant of Henei.
translations.idiomatic: When he arrested and prosecuted Zhong, the son of Lord Xiucheng and grandson of the Empress Dowager through her daughter, the emperor was impressed by his boldness and promoted him to Commandant of Henei.

sentences.zh: 至則族滅其豪穰氏之屬，河內道不拾遺。

translations.literal: Upon arriving, he exterminated the powerful Rang clan and their associates; on the roads of Henei, no one picked up lost items.
translations.idiomatic: Upon his arrival, he wiped out the powerful Rang clan and their associates. On the roads of Henei, no one so much as picked up what others had dropped.

sentences.zh: 而張次公亦為郎，以勇悍從軍，敢深入，有功，為岸頭侯。

translations.literal: Zhang Cigong also served as a Gentleman; by his bravery and ferocity in following the army, daring to press deep into enemy territory, he won merit and was enfeoffed as Marquis of Antou.
translations.idiomatic: Meanwhile, Zhang Cigong likewise served as a Gentleman. Through his bravery and ferocity on campaign, daring to press deep into enemy territory, he won distinction and was enfeoffed as the Marquis of Antou.

sentences.zh: 寧成家居，上欲以為郡守。

translations.literal: Ning Cheng was living at home, and the emperor wished to appoint him as a commandery governor.
translations.idiomatic: Ning Cheng was at that time living in retirement, and the emperor wished to appoint him as a commandery governor.

sentences.zh: 御史大夫弘曰：「臣居山東為小吏時，寧成為濟南都尉，其治如狼牧羊。

translations.literal: Imperial Secretary Hong said: 'When your servant served as a minor official east of the mountains, Ning Cheng was Commandant of Jinan; his governance was like a wolf herding sheep.'
translations.idiomatic: Imperial Secretary Gongsun Hong objected: "When I was a minor official east of the mountains, Ning Cheng was Commandant of Jinan. His governance was like a wolf set to herd sheep.

sentences.zh: 成不可使治民。」

translations.literal: Cheng must not be allowed to govern the people.
translations.idiomatic: Ning Cheng must not be placed in charge of the people."

sentences.zh: 上乃拜成為關都尉。

translations.literal: The emperor then appointed Cheng as Commandant of the Pass.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor therefore appointed Ning Cheng as Commandant of the Pass instead.

sentences.zh: 歲餘，關東吏隸郡國出入關者，號曰「寧見乳虎，無值寧成之怒」。

translations.literal: After a year or more, the officials and commoners from the eastern commanderies and kingdoms passing through the pass had a saying: 'Better to meet a nursing tigress than to encounter Ning Cheng in a rage.'
translations.idiomatic: After a little over a year, the officials and commoners from the eastern commanderies passing through the pass had a saying: "Better to face a tigress nursing her cubs than to cross Ning Cheng in a rage."

sentences.zh: 義縱自河內遷為南陽太守，聞寧成家居南陽，及縱至關，寧成側行送迎，然縱氣盛，弗為禮。

translations.literal: Yi Zong was transferred from Henei to become Grand Administrator of Nanyang. Hearing that Ning Cheng lived in Nanyang, when Zong arrived at the pass, Ning Cheng walked sideways to welcome and see him off; yet Zong, full of spirit, showed him no courtesy.
translations.idiomatic: Yi Zong was transferred from Henei to serve as Grand Administrator of Nanyang. He had heard that Ning Cheng was living in Nanyang. When Yi Zong passed through the gate, Ning Cheng sidled up to welcome and escort him, but Yi Zong, full of high spirits, showed him no courtesy.

sentences.zh: 至郡，遂案寧氏，盡破碎其家。

translations.literal: Upon arriving in the commandery, he prosecuted the Ning clan and utterly destroyed their household.
translations.idiomatic: Upon reaching the commandery, he launched an investigation of the Ning clan and utterly destroyed their household.

sentences.zh: 成坐有罪，及孔、暴之屬皆奔亡，南陽吏民重足一跡。

translations.literal: Cheng was convicted of crimes, and the Kong and Bao clans and their associates all fled; the officials and people of Nanyang placed one foot carefully upon the other.
translations.idiomatic: Ning Cheng was convicted of crimes, and the Kong and Bao clans and their associates all fled. The officials and people of Nanyang walked on tiptoe, scarcely daring to breathe.

sentences.zh: 而平氏朱彊、杜衍、杜周為縱牙爪之吏，任用，遷為廷史。

translations.literal: Zhu Qiang of Pingshi, Du Yan, and Du Zhou served as Yi Zong's trusted enforcers; they were given positions and promoted to Court Historians.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Qiang of Pingshi, Du Yan, and Du Zhou served as Yi Zong's trusted enforcers. They were appointed to positions of authority and later promoted to Court Clerks.

sentences.zh: 軍數出定襄，定襄吏民亂敗，於是徙縱為定襄太守。

translations.literal: The army had made several campaigns from Dingxiang, and the officials and people of Dingxiang had fallen into disorder; thereupon Yi Zong was transferred to serve as Grand Administrator of Dingxiang.
translations.idiomatic: The army had launched several campaigns from Dingxiang, and the officials and people there had fallen into disorder. Yi Zong was therefore transferred to serve as Grand Administrator of Dingxiang.

sentences.zh: 縱至，掩定襄獄中重罪輕系二百餘人，及賓客昆弟私入相視亦二百餘人。

translations.literal: When Zong arrived, he rounded up over two hundred people in the Dingxiang prison who had committed serious crimes but were only lightly detained, as well as over two hundred friends and relatives who had privately entered the prison to visit them.
translations.idiomatic: Upon his arrival, Yi Zong rounded up over two hundred prisoners in Dingxiang who had committed serious crimes but were only lightly detained, along with over two hundred friends and relatives who had slipped into the prison to visit them.

sentences.zh: 縱一捕鞠，曰「為死罪解脫」。

translations.literal: Zong arrested and tried them all in one sweep, declaring them guilty of 'aiding the escape of capital criminals.'
translations.idiomatic: He arrested and tried them all in a single sweep, charging them with "aiding the escape of capital criminals."

sentences.zh: 是日皆報殺四百餘人。

translations.literal: On that day, over four hundred people were sentenced to death.
translations.idiomatic: On that single day, over four hundred people were sentenced and put to death.

sentences.zh: 其后郡中不寒而栗，猾民佐吏為治。

translations.literal: Afterward, the whole commandery shivered without being cold, and the cunning among the people assisted the officials in maintaining order.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward, the entire commandery trembled though it was not cold, and even the most cunning of the populace rushed to assist the officials in keeping order.

sentences.zh: 是時趙禹、張湯以深刻為九卿矣，然其治尚寬，輔法而行，而縱以鷹擊毛摯為治。

translations.literal: At this time, Zhao Yu and Zhang Tang had already risen to the rank of the Nine Ministers through their harsh severity, yet their governance was still comparatively moderate, following the law as a guide; Zong, however, governed like a hawk striking and a bird of prey seizing.
translations.idiomatic: By this time, Zhao Yu and Zhang Tang had already risen to the rank of the Nine Ministers through their severe methods, yet their governance was comparatively moderate, using the law as a guide. Yi Zong, however, governed like a hawk swooping down upon its prey.

sentences.zh: 后會五銖錢白金起，民為姦，京師尤甚，乃以縱為右內史，王溫舒為中尉。

translations.literal: Later, when the five-zhu coin and white-metal currency were introduced and the people turned to crime, especially in the capital, Zong was appointed Right Prefect of the Capital and Wang Wenshu was made Commandant of Justice.
translations.idiomatic: Later, with the introduction of the five-zhu coin and the white-metal currency, the people turned to crime — particularly in the capital. Yi Zong was thereupon appointed Right Prefect of the Capital, and Wang Wenshu was made Commandant of Justice.

sentences.zh: 溫舒至惡，其所為不先言縱，縱必以氣淩之，敗壞其功。

translations.literal: Wenshu was extremely ruthless; whenever he acted without first consulting Zong, Zong would invariably assert his authority over him and undermine his accomplishments.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Wenshu was extremely ruthless. Whenever he took action without first consulting Yi Zong, Yi Zong would invariably pull rank on him and sabotage his efforts.

sentences.zh: 其治，所誅殺甚多，然取為小治，姦益不勝，直指始出矣。

translations.literal: In their governance, the number executed was very great, yet they achieved only minor order; crime proliferated beyond control, and the system of Direct Commissioners was first established.
translations.idiomatic: Under their governance, the number of those executed was enormous, yet they achieved only a semblance of order. Crime continued to multiply beyond control, and it was at this time that the system of Imperial Commissioners was first established.

sentences.zh: 吏之治以斬殺縛束為務，閻奉以惡用矣。

translations.literal: Officials made execution, slaughter, and imprisonment their main duty; Yan Feng was employed for his cruelty.
translations.idiomatic: Officials made execution, slaughter, and imprisonment their chief business. Yan Feng was put to use precisely for his cruelty.

sentences.zh: 縱廉，其治放郅都。

translations.literal: Zong was incorruptible; his governance resembled that of Zhi Du.
translations.idiomatic: Yi Zong was incorruptible, and his style of governance echoed that of Zhi Du.

sentences.zh: 上幸鼎湖，病久，已而卒起幸甘泉，道多不治。

translations.literal: The emperor visited Dinghu and was ill for a long time; afterward he suddenly arose and went to Ganquan, but found many parts of the road in disrepair.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor visited the Dinghu Palace and fell ill for an extended period. When he eventually recovered and set out for the Ganquan Palace, he found much of the road in disrepair.

sentences.zh: 上怒曰：「縱以我為不復行此道乎？」

translations.literal: The emperor said angrily: 'Does Zong think I shall never travel this road again?'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said in fury: "Does Yi Zong think I shall never travel this road again?"

sentences.zh: 嗛之。

translations.literal: He bore a grudge against him.
translations.idiomatic: He bore a grudge against him from that moment.

sentences.zh: 至冬，楊可方受告緡，縱以為此亂民，部吏捕其為可使者。

translations.literal: When winter came, Yang Ke was receiving reports of concealed wealth; Zong considered this to be disrupting the people, and dispatched his officials to arrest those who worked as Yang Ke's agents.
translations.idiomatic: When winter came, Yang Ke was collecting reports of concealed wealth. Yi Zong considered this a disruption to the people and dispatched his officials to arrest those serving as Yang Ke's agents.

sentences.zh: 天子聞，使杜式治，以為廢格沮事，棄縱市。

translations.literal: When the Son of Heaven heard of this, he sent Du Shi to investigate. Yi Zong was found guilty of obstructing and sabotaging state affairs, and he was executed in the marketplace.
translations.idiomatic: When the Son of Heaven heard of this, he sent Du Shi to investigate. Yi Zong was found guilty of obstructing and sabotaging state affairs and was executed in the marketplace.

sentences.zh: 後一歲，張湯亦死。

translations.literal: A year later, Zhang Tang also died.
translations.idiomatic: A year later, Zhang Tang likewise met his end.

sentences.zh: 王溫舒者，陽陵人也。

translations.literal: Wang Wenshu was a man from Yangling.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Wenshu was a native of Yangling.

sentences.zh: 少時椎埋為姦。

translations.literal: In his youth he robbed graves for a living.
translations.idiomatic: In his youth, he robbed graves for a living.

sentences.zh: 已而試補縣亭長，數廢。

translations.literal: Later he was tried out and appointed as a district head, but was dismissed several times.
translations.idiomatic: Later he was appointed as a district head on a trial basis, but was dismissed several times.

sentences.zh: 為吏，以治獄至廷史。

translations.literal: As an official, through handling legal cases he rose to the rank of Court Clerk.
translations.idiomatic: As an official, he rose through handling legal cases to the rank of Court Clerk.

sentences.zh: 事張湯，遷為御史。

translations.literal: He served under Zhang Tang and was promoted to Censor.
translations.idiomatic: He served under Zhang Tang and was promoted to Censor.

sentences.zh: 督盜賊，殺傷甚多，稍遷至廣平都尉。

translations.literal: He supervised the suppression of bandits and thieves, killing and wounding a great many; he was gradually promoted to Commandant of Guangping.
translations.idiomatic: He oversaw the suppression of bandits and thieves, killing and wounding a great many. He was gradually promoted to Commandant of Guangping.

sentences.zh: 擇郡中豪敢任吏十餘人，以為爪牙，皆把其陰重罪，而縱使督盜賊，快其意所欲得。

translations.literal: He selected over ten bold and capable men from the local strongmen of the commandery to serve as his enforcers, holding evidence of their grave secret crimes, and unleashed them to suppress bandits and thieves, satisfying whatever they desired.
translations.idiomatic: He selected over ten bold and capable men from among the local strongmen of the commandery to serve as his enforcers. He held evidence of their secret crimes and turned them loose to suppress bandits and thieves, letting them indulge their every desire.

sentences.zh: 此人雖有百罪，弗法；

translations.literal: Even if these men committed a hundred crimes, he would not prosecute them;
translations.idiomatic: Even if these men committed a hundred crimes, he would not prosecute them;

sentences.zh: 即有避，因其事夷之，亦滅宗。

translations.literal: But if any tried to evade him, he would use their crimes to destroy them, wiping out their entire clan.
translations.idiomatic: but if any tried to evade his orders, he would use the evidence against them to destroy them, wiping out their entire clan.

sentences.zh: 以其故齊趙之郊盜賊不敢近廣平，廣平聲為道不拾遺。

translations.literal: Because of this, bandits and thieves from the borders of Qi and Zhao dared not approach Guangping, and Guangping gained the reputation that nothing was picked up from the road.
translations.idiomatic: As a result, the bandits of the Qi and Zhao borderlands dared not venture near Guangping, and the commandery gained the reputation that not a thing was picked up from its roads.

sentences.zh: 上聞，遷為河內太守。

translations.literal: The emperor heard of this and promoted him to Grand Administrator of Henei.
translations.idiomatic: When the emperor heard of this, he promoted Wang Wenshu to Grand Administrator of Henei.

sentences.zh: 素居廣平時，皆知河內豪姦之家，及往，九月而至。

translations.literal: While previously in Guangping, he had already learned of all the powerful and criminal families in Henei; when he went there, he arrived in the ninth month.
translations.idiomatic: During his time in Guangping, he had already identified all the powerful criminal families in Henei. He set out for his new post and arrived in the ninth month.

sentences.zh: 令郡具私馬五十匹，為驛自河內至長安，部吏如居廣平時方略，捕郡中豪猾，郡中豪猾相連坐千餘家。

translations.literal: He ordered the commandery to provide fifty private horses, set up a relay post from Henei to Chang'an, deployed his officials according to the same methods as in Guangping, and arrested the powerful and lawless of the commandery; over a thousand families were implicated through collective guilt.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered the commandery to furnish fifty private horses and established a relay post from Henei to Chang'an. He deployed his officials using the same tactics as in Guangping and arrested the powerful and lawless families of the commandery. Over a thousand families were implicated through the system of collective guilt.

sentences.zh: 上書請，大者至族，小者乃死，家盡沒入償臧。

translations.literal: He memorialized the emperor requesting that the worst offenders be executed with their entire clans, and the lesser offenders put to death, with all their family property confiscated to compensate for the stolen goods.
translations.idiomatic: He memorialized the emperor requesting that the worst offenders be executed along with their entire clans, the lesser offenders put to death, and all their family property confiscated to make restitution.

sentences.zh: 奏行不過二三日，得可事。

translations.literal: His memorials reached the capital in no more than two or three days and received approval.
translations.idiomatic: His memorials reached the capital in no more than two or three days and received prompt approval.

sentences.zh: 論報，至流血十餘里。

translations.literal: When sentences were carried out, blood flowed for more than ten li.
translations.idiomatic: When the sentences were carried out, blood flowed for more than ten li.

sentences.zh: 河內皆怪其奏，以為神速。

translations.literal: All of Henei marveled at how quickly his memorials received replies, considering them supernaturally fast.
translations.idiomatic: All of Henei marveled at the speed with which his memorials were answered, calling it supernaturally swift.

sentences.zh: 盡十二月，郡中毋聲，毋敢夜行，野無犬吠之盜。

translations.literal: By the end of the twelfth month, the commandery was silent; no one dared walk at night, and in the countryside there were no thieves even for dogs to bark at.
translations.idiomatic: By the end of the twelfth month, the commandery had fallen silent. No one dared go out at night, and in the countryside there were not even thieves for the dogs to bark at.

sentences.zh: 其頗不得，失之旁郡國，黎來，會春，溫舒頓足嘆曰：「嗟乎，令冬月益展一月，足吾事矣！」

translations.literal: Some he could not catch and they escaped to neighboring commanderies and kingdoms; when spring arrived, Wenshu stamped his feet and sighed: 'Alas! If only winter were extended by one more month, I could have finished the job!'
translations.idiomatic: Some of the fugitives escaped to neighboring commanderies and kingdoms. When spring arrived, Wang Wenshu stamped his feet and sighed: "Alas! If only winter had lasted one month longer, I could have finished the job!"

sentences.zh: 其好殺伐行威不愛人如此。

translations.literal: Such was his love of killing and wielding authority with no care for human life.
translations.idiomatic: Such was his love of killing and wielding authority, with no regard for human life.

sentences.zh: 天子聞之，以為能，遷為中尉。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven heard of this, considered him capable, and promoted him to Commandant of Justice.
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven, hearing of this and considering him capable, promoted him to Commandant of Justice.

sentences.zh: 其治復放河內，徙諸名禍猾吏與從事，河內則楊皆、麻戊，關中楊贛、成信等。

translations.literal: His governance again followed the methods used in Henei; he transferred notorious troublemaking officials to work with him — from Henei, Yang Jie and Ma Wu; from within the passes, Yang Gan and Cheng Xin, among others.
translations.idiomatic: His methods followed the same pattern as in Henei. He transferred notorious and ruthless officials to serve under him — from Henei came Yang Jie and Ma Wu; from within the passes, Yang Gan and Cheng Xin, among others.

sentences.zh: 義縱為內史，憚未敢恣治。

translations.literal: Yi Zong was serving as Prefect of the Capital, and Wenshu, fearing him, did not yet dare act without restraint.
translations.idiomatic: Yi Zong was at that time serving as Prefect of the Capital, and Wang Wenshu, fearing him, did not yet dare give free rein to his methods.

sentences.zh: 及縱死，張湯敗後，徙為廷尉，而尹齊為中尉。

translations.literal: When Zong died and Zhang Tang was disgraced, Wenshu was transferred to Commandant of Trials, and Yin Qi became Commandant of Justice.
translations.idiomatic: After Yi Zong's death and Zhang Tang's disgrace, Wang Wenshu was transferred to the post of Commandant of Trials, while Yin Qi became Commandant of Justice.

sentences.zh: 尹齊者，東郡茌平人。

translations.literal: Yin Qi was a man from Chiping in Dong Commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Yin Qi was a native of Chiping in Dong Commandery.

sentences.zh: 以刀筆稍遷至御史。

translations.literal: Through clerical work he was gradually promoted to Censor.
translations.idiomatic: Through his clerical work, he was gradually promoted to the rank of Censor.

sentences.zh: 事張湯，張湯數稱以為廉武，使督盜賊，所斬伐不避貴戚。

translations.literal: He served under Zhang Tang, who frequently praised him as incorruptible and forceful, and assigned him to suppress bandits and thieves. In his executions he spared neither the noble nor the wellborn.
translations.idiomatic: He served under Zhang Tang, who frequently praised him for his integrity and forcefulness and assigned him to suppress bandits and thieves. In carrying out executions, he spared neither the noble nor the wellborn.

sentences.zh: 遷為關內都尉，聲甚於寧成。

translations.literal: He was promoted to Commandant of Guannei; his reputation surpassed that of Ning Cheng.
translations.idiomatic: He was promoted to Commandant of Guannei, where his fearsome reputation surpassed even that of Ning Cheng.

sentences.zh: 上以為能，遷為中尉，吏民益凋敝。

translations.literal: The emperor considered him capable and promoted him to Commandant of Justice; the officials and people grew ever more impoverished and worn.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor considered him capable and promoted him to Commandant of Justice. Under his rule, the officials and people grew ever more wretched and broken.

sentences.zh: 尹齊木彊少文，豪惡吏伏匿而善吏不能為治，以故事多廢，抵罪。

translations.literal: Yin Qi was rigid and unrefined; the powerful and wicked officials went into hiding while the honest officials could not maintain order. As a result, many cases went unfinished, and he was convicted.
translations.idiomatic: Yin Qi was rigid and lacking in finesse. The powerful and corrupt officials went into hiding, while honest officials found themselves unable to govern effectively. As a result, many matters fell through, and Yin Qi was convicted of offenses.

sentences.zh: 上復徙溫舒為中尉，而楊仆以嚴酷為主爵都尉。

translations.literal: The emperor again transferred Wenshu to Commandant of Justice, and Yang Pu, on account of his severity and harshness, was made Commandant of Bestowing Nobility.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor once again transferred Wang Wenshu to the post of Commandant of Justice, while Yang Pu, known for his severity and cruelty, was appointed Commandant of Bestowing Nobility.

sentences.zh: 楊仆者，宜陽人也。

translations.literal: Yang Pu was a man from Yiyang.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Pu was a native of Yiyang.

sentences.zh: 以千夫為吏。

translations.literal: He entered office through the rank of a Thousand-man.
translations.idiomatic: He entered government service through the military rank of a Thousand-man.

sentences.zh: 河南守案舉以為能，遷為御史，使督盜賊關東。

translations.literal: The Governor of Henan investigated and recommended him as capable; he was promoted to Censor and assigned to suppress bandits and thieves east of the passes.
translations.idiomatic: The Governor of Henan examined his record, recommended him as capable, and he was promoted to Censor and assigned to suppress bandits and thieves east of the passes.

sentences.zh: 治放尹齊，以為敢摯行。

translations.literal: His governance resembled that of Yin Qi; he was considered bold and decisive in action.
translations.idiomatic: His methods of governance resembled those of Yin Qi; he was considered bold and resolute in action.

sentences.zh: 稍遷至主爵都尉，列九卿。

translations.literal: He was gradually promoted to Commandant of Bestowing Nobility, ranked among the Nine Ministers.
translations.idiomatic: He was gradually promoted to the rank of Commandant of Bestowing Nobility, joining the Nine Ministers.

sentences.zh: 天子以為能。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven considered him capable.
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven considered him a man of ability.

sentences.zh: 南越反，拜為樓船將軍，有功，封將梁侯。

translations.literal: When Nanyue rebelled, he was appointed General of the Towered Ships; he won merit and was enfeoffed as Marquis of Jiangliang.
translations.idiomatic: When Nanyue rebelled, he was appointed General of the Towered Ships. He distinguished himself in the campaign and was enfeoffed as the Marquis of Jiangliang.

sentences.zh: 為荀彘所縛。

translations.literal: He was bound by Xun Zhi.
translations.idiomatic: He was arrested and bound by Xun Zhi.

sentences.zh: 居久之，病死。

translations.literal: After a long time, he died of illness.
translations.idiomatic: Some time later, he died of illness.

sentences.zh: 而溫舒復為中尉。

translations.literal: Wang Wenshu again became Commandant of Justice.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Wenshu was once again appointed Commandant of Justice.

sentences.zh: 為人少文，居廷惛惛不辯，至於中尉則心開。

translations.literal: He was a man of little refinement; at court he appeared dull and inarticulate, but when serving as Commandant of Justice his mind came alive.
translations.idiomatic: He was a man of little refinement. At court he appeared dull and tongue-tied, but once in the role of Commandant of Justice, his mind came fully alive.

sentences.zh: 督盜賊，素習關中俗，知豪惡吏，豪惡吏盡復為用，為方略。

translations.literal: In suppressing bandits and thieves, he was long acquainted with the customs of Guanzhong and knew the powerful and wicked officials; these wicked officials were all put back to use, serving as his agents in his strategies.
translations.idiomatic: In suppressing bandits and thieves, he drew on his long familiarity with the customs of the Guanzhong region and his knowledge of the local strongmen and corrupt officials. He put all these men back to work as agents in his schemes.

sentences.zh: 吏苛察，盜賊惡少年投缿購告言姦，置伯格長以牧司姦盜賊。

translations.literal: His officials conducted rigorous investigations; young toughs and petty thieves dropped accusations into the reporting boxes offering bounties, informing on criminals. He appointed local patrol leaders to monitor and oversee criminals, bandits, and thieves.
translations.idiomatic: His officials conducted rigorous investigations. Young toughs and petty thieves dropped denunciations into the bounty boxes, informing on criminals. He appointed patrol leaders throughout the districts to monitor and track down criminals, bandits, and thieves.

sentences.zh: 溫舒為人讇善事有埶者；

translations.literal: Wenshu was a man who flattered and served those with power;
translations.idiomatic: Wang Wenshu was by nature a flatterer who curried favor with those in power;

sentences.zh: 即無埶者，視之如奴。

translations.literal: Those without power he treated as slaves.
translations.idiomatic: those without power he treated like slaves.

sentences.zh: 有埶家，雖有姦如山，弗犯；

translations.literal: Against powerful families, even if their crimes were piled high as a mountain, he would not touch them;
translations.idiomatic: The crimes of powerful families might be piled high as mountains, yet he would not touch them;

sentences.zh: 無埶者，貴戚必侵辱。

translations.literal: Those without power, even if they were of noble birth, he would invariably oppress and humiliate.
translations.idiomatic: but those without power, even if they were of noble birth, he invariably oppressed and humiliated.

sentences.zh: 舞文巧詆下戶之猾，以熏大豪。

translations.literal: He twisted the law and crafted clever accusations against the cunning among the poor, in order to terrify the great powerful families.
translations.idiomatic: He twisted the law to craft ingenious accusations against the petty swindlers among the poor, using them to strike fear into the great and powerful.

sentences.zh: 其治中尉如此。

translations.literal: Such was his governance as Commandant of Justice.
translations.idiomatic: Such was his manner of governing as Commandant of Justice.

sentences.zh: 姦猾窮治，大抵盡靡爛獄中，行論無出者。

translations.literal: The cunning and lawless were prosecuted to the end; nearly all rotted away in prison, and of those sentenced, none came out alive.
translations.idiomatic: Those he deemed cunning and lawless were prosecuted to the bitter end. Nearly all of them rotted away in prison, and of those sentenced, not one emerged alive.

sentences.zh: 其爪牙吏虎而冠。

translations.literal: His enforcer-officials were tigers wearing caps.
translations.idiomatic: His enforcer-officials were tigers wearing the caps of men.

sentences.zh: 於是中尉部中中猾以下皆伏，有勢者為游聲譽，稱治。

translations.literal: Thus, within the Commandant's jurisdiction, the middling and petty criminals all submitted; the powerful spread favorable reports of his reputation, praising his governance.
translations.idiomatic: Within the Commandant's jurisdiction, all the middling and petty criminals were cowed into submission. The powerful, meanwhile, spread favorable reports of his reputation and praised his governance.

sentences.zh: 治數歲，其吏多以權富。

translations.literal: After governing for several years, many of his officials grew wealthy through their power.
translations.idiomatic: After several years of his rule, many of his officials grew wealthy through the power they wielded.

sentences.zh: 溫舒擊東越還，議有不中意者，坐小法抵罪免。

translations.literal: When Wenshu returned from the campaign against Eastern Yue, his proposals displeased the emperor; he was convicted of a minor legal offense and dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: When Wang Wenshu returned from the campaign against the Eastern Yue, certain of his proposals displeased the emperor. He was convicted of a minor legal infraction and dismissed from office.

sentences.zh: 是時天子方欲作通天臺而未有人，溫舒請覆中尉脫卒，得數萬人作。

translations.literal: At this time the Son of Heaven wished to build the Tower Reaching to Heaven but had no labor force. Wenshu requested to review the Commandant's rolls and found that tens of thousands of soldiers had deserted their service; he put them to work.
translations.idiomatic: At that time, the Son of Heaven wished to build the Tower Reaching to Heaven but lacked the labor force. Wang Wenshu proposed reviewing the Commandant's military rolls for deserters and managed to round up tens of thousands of men to put to work.

sentences.zh: 上說，拜為少府。

translations.literal: The emperor was pleased and appointed him Privy Treasurer.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor was delighted and appointed him Privy Treasurer.

sentences.zh: 徙為右內史，治如其故，姦邪少禁。

translations.literal: He was transferred to Right Prefect of the Capital; his governance followed his old methods, and crime was little restrained.
translations.idiomatic: He was transferred to the post of Right Prefect of the Capital. He governed in his usual fashion, and crime was scarcely kept in check.

sentences.zh: 坐法失官。

translations.literal: He was convicted of a legal offense and lost his post.
translations.idiomatic: He was convicted of a legal offense and stripped of his post.

sentences.zh: 復為右輔，行中尉事。

translations.literal: He was again appointed as Right Assistant and acted as Commandant of Justice.
translations.idiomatic: He was reappointed as Right Assistant and given charge of the Commandant of Justice's duties.

sentences.zh: 如故操。

translations.literal: He governed as before.
translations.idiomatic: He carried on exactly as before.

sentences.zh: 歲餘，會宛軍發，詔徵豪吏，溫舒匿其吏華成，及人有變告溫舒受員騎錢，他姦利事，罪至族，自殺。

translations.literal: After a year or more, when the army was dispatched against Dayuan, an edict summoned powerful officials to service. Wenshu concealed his official Hua Cheng, and then someone submitted an accusation that Wenshu had accepted bribes from cavalrymen and committed other corrupt profiteering. His crimes warranted the execution of his clan, and he killed himself.
translations.idiomatic: After about a year, when the army was dispatched against Dayuan, an imperial edict summoned powerful officials for service. Wang Wenshu concealed his subordinate Hua Cheng from the call-up. Then someone submitted an accusation that Wang Wenshu had accepted bribes from cavalrymen, along with other corrupt dealings. His crimes warranted the execution of his entire clan, and he took his own life.

sentences.zh: 其時兩弟及兩婚家亦各自坐他罪而族。

translations.literal: At the same time, his two brothers and two families related by marriage were each convicted of separate crimes and had their clans exterminated.
translations.idiomatic: At the same time, his two brothers and the families of two in-laws were each independently convicted of separate crimes and had their clans wiped out.

sentences.zh: 光祿徐自為曰：「悲夫，夫古有三族，而王溫舒罪至同時而五族乎！」

translations.literal: The Counselor of the Palace, Xu Ziwei, remarked: 'How tragic! In ancient times there was the punishment of the three clans, but Wang Wenshu's crimes led to the simultaneous destruction of five clans!'
translations.idiomatic: The Counselor of the Palace, Xu Ziwei, remarked: "How tragic! In ancient times, the punishment of the three clans was considered extreme. Yet Wang Wenshu's crimes brought about the simultaneous destruction of five clans!"

sentences.zh: 溫舒死，家直累千金。

translations.literal: When Wenshu died, his family's estate was worth several thousand catties of gold.
translations.idiomatic: When Wang Wenshu died, his family estate was worth several thousand catties of gold.

sentences.zh: 後數歲，尹齊亦以淮陽都尉病死，家直不滿五十金。

translations.literal: Several years later, Yin Qi also died of illness while serving as Commandant of Huaiyang; his family's estate was worth less than fifty catties of gold.
translations.idiomatic: Several years later, Yin Qi likewise died of illness while serving as Commandant of Huaiyang. His family estate was worth less than fifty catties of gold.

sentences.zh: 所誅滅淮陽甚多，及死，仇家欲燒其尸，尸亡去歸葬。

translations.literal: He had executed and destroyed a great many people in Huaiyang; upon his death, the families of his victims wished to burn his corpse. The body was spirited away and secretly taken back for burial.
translations.idiomatic: He had put a great many people to death in Huaiyang, and upon his death, the families of his victims wanted to burn his corpse. The body had to be spirited away and taken back in secret for burial.

sentences.zh: 自溫舒等以惡為治，而郡守、都尉、諸侯二千石欲為治者，其治大抵盡放溫舒，而吏民益輕犯法，盜賊滋起。

translations.literal: After Wenshu and others used cruelty as their method of governance, the commandery governors, commandants, and two-thousand-picul officials of the feudal kingdoms who aspired to govern well all modeled their governance on Wenshu's. Yet officials and people increasingly made light of the law, and bandits and thieves multiplied.
translations.idiomatic: After Wang Wenshu and his ilk made cruelty their governing principle, the commandery governors, commandants, and officials of two-thousand-picul rank throughout the feudal kingdoms who aspired to effective governance all modeled themselves on Wang Wenshu. Yet officials and common people alike grew ever more contemptuous of the law, and bandits and thieves multiplied.

sentences.zh: 南陽有梅免、白政，楚有殷中、杜少，齊有徐勃，燕趙之閒有堅盧、范生之屬。

translations.literal: In Nanyang there were Mei Mian and Bai Zheng; in Chu, Yin Zhong and Du Shao; in Qi, Xu Bo; and between Yan and Zhao, Jian Lu, Fan Sheng, and their like.
translations.idiomatic: In Nanyang there arose Mei Mian and Bai Zheng; in Chu, Yin Zhong and Du Shao; in Qi, Xu Bo; and in the lands between Yan and Zhao, Jian Lu, Fan Sheng, and their kind.

sentences.zh: 大群至數千人，擅自號，攻城邑，取庫兵，釋死罪，縛辱郡太守、都尉，殺二千石，為檄告縣趣具食；

translations.literal: The largest bands numbered in the thousands; they assumed their own titles, attacked cities and towns, seized weapons from the armories, freed those condemned to death, bound and humiliated Grand Administrators and Commandants, killed two-thousand-picul officials, and issued proclamations ordering the counties to prepare food;
translations.idiomatic: The largest bands numbered in the thousands. They assumed their own titles, assaulted cities and towns, seized weapons from the armories, freed condemned prisoners, bound and humiliated the Grand Administrators and Commandants, killed officials of two-thousand-picul rank, and issued proclamations ordering the counties to provide them with food.

sentences.zh: 小群以百數，掠鹵鄉里者，不可勝數也。

translations.literal: Smaller bands numbered in the hundreds; those who plundered the villages and countryside were beyond counting.
translations.idiomatic: The smaller bands numbered in the hundreds, and those who plundered the villages and countryside were beyond counting.

sentences.zh: 於是天子始使御史中丞、丞相長史督之。

translations.literal: Thereupon the Son of Heaven first dispatched the Vice Censor and the Chancellor's Senior Clerk to oversee the suppression.
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven first dispatched the Vice Censor and the Chancellor's Senior Clerk to oversee the suppression efforts.

sentences.zh: 猶弗能禁也，乃使光祿大夫范昆、諸輔都尉及故九卿張德等衣繡衣，持節，虎符發兵以興擊，斬首大部或至萬餘級，及以法誅通飲食，坐連諸郡，甚者數千人。

translations.literal: Yet still they could not suppress it. So they sent the Counselor Grandee Fan Kun, the various Assistant Commandants, and former Nine Minister Zhang De, all wearing embroidered garments and bearing the imperial tally and tiger tallies, to mobilize troops and launch attacks. In the major engagements, over ten thousand heads were sometimes taken; those executed under the law for harboring or feeding the bandits, and those implicated through the system of collective guilt across the commanderies, numbered in the thousands at worst.
translations.idiomatic: Yet still they could not suppress the disorder. The emperor then dispatched the Counselor Grandee Fan Kun, the various Assistant Commandants, and the former Nine Minister Zhang De — all wearing embroidered garments and bearing the imperial tally and tiger tallies — to mobilize troops and launch attacks. In the major campaigns, more than ten thousand heads were sometimes taken. Those executed under the law for harboring or providing food to the bandits, along with those implicated through collective guilt across the commanderies, numbered in the thousands.

sentences.zh: 數歲，乃頗得其渠率。

translations.literal: After several years, they gradually captured the ringleaders.
translations.idiomatic: After several years, the ringleaders were at last captured.

sentences.zh: 散卒失亡，復聚黨阻山川者，往往而群居，無可柰何。

translations.literal: Scattered soldiers and fugitives would reassemble, forming gangs and fortifying themselves among mountains and rivers, clustering together in groups everywhere — nothing could be done about them.
translations.idiomatic: Scattered soldiers and fugitives would regroup, form new bands, and take refuge among the mountains and rivers, gathering together everywhere. Nothing could be done about them.

sentences.zh: 於是作「沈命法」，曰群盜起不發覺，發覺而捕弗滿品者，二千石以下至小吏主者皆死。

translations.literal: Thereupon the 'Law of Sunken Fates' was enacted, stating that if gangs of bandits arose and were not discovered, or were discovered but not apprehended in sufficient numbers, all officials from the two-thousand-picul rank down to the lowest clerk responsible would be put to death.
translations.idiomatic: Thereupon the "Law of Sunken Fates" was enacted, which decreed that if bandit gangs arose and went undetected, or were detected but not captured in the required numbers, all officials responsible — from those of two-thousand-picul rank down to the humblest clerk — would be put to death.

sentences.zh: 其後小吏畏誅，雖有盜不敢發，恐不能得，坐課累府，府亦使其不言。

translations.literal: After this, minor officials feared execution and dared not report bandits even when they existed, lest they fail to capture them and be punished under the quota system, burdening the higher offices; the higher offices also directed them not to report.
translations.idiomatic: After this, minor officials feared for their lives and dared not report bandits even when they knew of them, lest they fail to meet their capture quotas and be punished. The higher offices, in turn, instructed them to keep silent.

sentences.zh: 故盜賊寖多，上下相為匿，以文辭避法焉。

translations.literal: Thus bandits and thieves grew ever more numerous, officials at every level concealed their existence for one another, and used legal wording to evade the law.
translations.idiomatic: As a result, bandits and thieves multiplied. Officials at every level covered for one another and used the letter of the law to evade its enforcement.

sentences.zh: 減宣者，楊人也。

translations.literal: Jian Xuan was a man from Yang.
translations.idiomatic: Jian Xuan was a native of Yang.

sentences.zh: 以佐史無害給事河東守府。

translations.literal: On account of his ability as an Assistant Clerk, he was assigned to serve in the Hedong Governor's office.
translations.idiomatic: On account of his ability as an Assistant Clerk, he was assigned to serve in the Governor's office in Hedong.

sentences.zh: 衛將軍青使買馬河東，見宣無害，言上，徵為大廐丞。

translations.literal: General-in-Chief Wei Qing, on a mission to purchase horses in Hedong, noticed Xuan's ability and recommended him to the emperor. He was summoned to serve as Vice Director of the Imperial Stables.
translations.idiomatic: General-in-Chief Wei Qing, on a mission to purchase horses in Hedong, noticed Jian Xuan's ability and recommended him to the emperor. He was summoned to the capital to serve as Vice Director of the Imperial Stables.

sentences.zh: 官事辨，稍遷至御史及中丞。

translations.literal: He handled official affairs efficiently and was gradually promoted to Censor and then Vice Censor.
translations.idiomatic: He proved efficient in handling official affairs and was gradually promoted to Censor and then Vice Censor.

sentences.zh: 使治主父偃及治淮南反獄，所以微文深詆，殺者甚眾，稱為敢決疑。

translations.literal: He was assigned to handle the case of Zhufu Yan and the Huainan rebellion case. Through subtle legal manipulation and severe accusations, he put many to death and earned the reputation of being bold in resolving doubtful cases.
translations.idiomatic: He was assigned to handle the case of Zhufu Yan and the Huainan rebellion case. Through subtle legal manipulation and severe accusations, he put a great many to death and earned the reputation of being bold in resolving doubtful cases.

sentences.zh: 數廢數起，為御史及中丞者幾二十歲。

translations.literal: He was dismissed and reinstated several times, serving as Censor and Vice Censor for nearly twenty years.
translations.idiomatic: He was dismissed and reinstated several times, serving as Censor and Vice Censor for nearly twenty years in all.

sentences.zh: 王溫舒免中尉，而宣為左內史。

translations.literal: When Wang Wenshu was dismissed as Commandant of Justice, Xuan was appointed Left Prefect of the Capital.
translations.idiomatic: When Wang Wenshu was dismissed from the post of Commandant of Justice, Jian Xuan was appointed Left Prefect of the Capital.

sentences.zh: 其治米鹽，事大小皆關其手，自部署縣名曹實物，官吏令丞不得擅搖，痛以重法繩之。

translations.literal: His governance was meticulous; affairs both great and small all passed through his hands. He personally organized the county departments and their inventory of goods and properties, and officials from the county magistrate down to the clerks were forbidden to act independently, bound with severe laws.
translations.idiomatic: His governance was meticulous to the last detail. Affairs both great and small all passed through his own hands. He personally organized the county departments and their inventories, forbade every official from county magistrate to clerk from acting independently, and bound them all with the harshest laws.

sentences.zh: 居官數年，一切郡中為小治辨，然獨宣以小致大，能因力行之，難以為經。

translations.literal: After governing for several years, the entire commandery achieved a measure of order and efficiency. Yet only Xuan could build something large from something small, carrying it through by force of will; this was difficult to sustain as a permanent system.
translations.idiomatic: After several years in office, the entire commandery achieved a measure of order and efficiency. Yet only Jian Xuan's personal force of will could build so much from so little — it was a system impossible to sustain as a permanent model.

sentences.zh: 中廢。

translations.literal: He was dismissed from office.
translations.idiomatic: He was eventually dismissed.

sentences.zh: 為右扶風，坐怨成信，信亡藏上林中，宣使郿令格殺信，吏卒格信時，射中上林苑門，宣下吏詆罪，以為大逆，當族，自殺。

translations.literal: When serving as Right Fufeng, he harbored a grudge against Cheng Xin. When Xin fled and hid in the Shanglin Park, Xuan ordered the Magistrate of Mei to kill Xin. When the officials and soldiers fought Xin, their arrows struck the gate of the Shanglin Park. Xuan was handed over to the judicial officials and charged with a capital crime, deemed guilty of great treason warranting the execution of his clan. He killed himself.
translations.idiomatic: While serving as Right Fufeng, he bore a grudge against Cheng Xin. When Cheng Xin fled and hid in the Shanglin Park, Jian Xuan ordered the Magistrate of Mei to hunt him down and kill him. In the fighting, the soldiers' arrows struck the gate of the Shanglin Park. Jian Xuan was turned over to the judicial officials and charged with a capital crime — deemed guilty of high treason, warranting the execution of his entire clan. He took his own life.

sentences.zh: 而杜周任用。

translations.literal: And Du Zhou was appointed to office.
translations.idiomatic: Meanwhile, Du Zhou rose to prominence.

sentences.zh: 杜周者，南陽杜衍人。

translations.literal: Du Zhou was a man from Duyan in Nanyang.
translations.idiomatic: Du Zhou was a native of Duyan in Nanyang.

sentences.zh: 義縱為南陽守，以為爪牙，舉為廷尉史。

translations.literal: When Yi Zong was Governor of Nanyang, he employed Du Zhou as an enforcer and recommended him as a Clerk to the Commandant of Trials.
translations.idiomatic: When Yi Zong served as Governor of Nanyang, he used Du Zhou as one of his enforcers and recommended him for the position of Clerk to the Commandant of Trials.

sentences.zh: 事張湯，湯數言其無害，至御史。

translations.literal: He served under Zhang Tang, who frequently praised his ability, and he rose to the rank of Censor.
translations.idiomatic: He served under Zhang Tang, who frequently commended his ability. He was promoted to the rank of Censor.

sentences.zh: 使案邊失亡，所論殺甚眾。

translations.literal: He was sent to investigate losses and desertions on the frontier; the number he prosecuted and killed was very great.
translations.idiomatic: He was dispatched to investigate losses and desertions on the frontier. The number he prosecuted and put to death was enormous.

sentences.zh: 奏事中上意，任用，與減宣相編，更為中丞十餘歲。

translations.literal: His memorials met with the emperor's approval; he was given responsibilities and, alternating with Jian Xuan, served as Vice Censor for over ten years.
translations.idiomatic: His memorials consistently met with the emperor's approval. He was given increasing responsibilities and, alternating with Jian Xuan, served as Vice Censor for over ten years.

sentences.zh: 其治與宣相放，然重遲，外寬，內深次骨。

translations.literal: His governance resembled Xuan's, yet he was ponderous and slow; outwardly lenient, but inwardly his severity cut to the bone.
translations.idiomatic: His methods of governance resembled Jian Xuan's, yet he was deliberate and unhurried. Outwardly he appeared lenient, but inwardly his severity cut to the bone.

sentences.zh: 宣為左內史，周為廷尉，其治大放張湯而善候伺。

translations.literal: When Xuan served as Left Prefect of the Capital and Zhou as Commandant of Trials, Zhou's governance largely followed Zhang Tang's methods, excelling at watching and waiting.
translations.idiomatic: When Jian Xuan served as Left Prefect of the Capital and Du Zhou as Commandant of Trials, Du Zhou's methods largely followed Zhang Tang's example, though he excelled above all in watching and waiting.

sentences.zh: 上所欲擠者，因而陷之；

translations.literal: Those the emperor wished to push down, he would accordingly entrap;
translations.idiomatic: Whomever the emperor wished to destroy, he would duly entrap;

sentences.zh: 上所欲釋者，久系待問而微見其冤狀。

translations.literal: Those the emperor wished to release, he would hold in prison for a long time as if awaiting interrogation, then subtly reveal the signs of their innocence.
translations.idiomatic: whomever the emperor wished to release, he would hold in prison for a long time under the pretense of continued investigation, then subtly bring to light the signs of their innocence.

sentences.zh: 客有讓周曰：「君為天子決平，不循三尺法，專以人主意指為獄。

translations.literal: A guest once rebuked Zhou, saying: 'You adjudicate cases for the Son of Heaven, yet you do not follow the three-foot law, but decide cases solely according to the ruler's will.'
translations.idiomatic: A guest once rebuked Du Zhou: "You adjudicate cases on behalf of the Son of Heaven, yet you do not follow the three-foot tablets of the law, but decide cases solely according to the ruler's wishes.

sentences.zh: 獄者固如是乎？」

translations.literal: Is this truly how the law should be administered?
translations.idiomatic: Is this truly how justice should be administered?"

sentences.zh: 周曰：「三尺安出哉？

translations.literal: Zhou said: 'Where do the three-foot laws come from?'
translations.idiomatic: Du Zhou replied: "And where do you suppose those three-foot laws come from?

sentences.zh: 前主所是著為律，後主所是疏為令，當時為是，何古之法乎！」

translations.literal: What the former ruler approved was written as statute; what the present ruler approves is issued as decree. What matters is what is right at the time — what need is there for ancient laws!
translations.idiomatic: What the former ruler approved was written into statute; what the present ruler approves is issued as decree. What matters is what the ruler deems right at the time. What need is there for ancient laws?"

sentences.zh: 至周為廷尉，詔獄亦益多矣。

translations.literal: By the time Zhou served as Commandant of Trials, the number of cases brought by imperial decree had grown ever more numerous.
translations.idiomatic: By the time Du Zhou served as Commandant of Trials, the number of cases initiated by imperial decree had grown enormously.

sentences.zh: 二千石系者新故相因，不減百餘人。

translations.literal: Among those imprisoned with salaries of two thousand shi, old and new cases overlapped continuously, never numbering fewer than over a hundred.
translations.idiomatic: Among the imprisoned officials with salaries of two thousand shi, old and new cases overlapped endlessly, and the count never fell below a hundred.

sentences.zh: 郡吏大府舉之廷尉，一歲至千餘章。

translations.literal: Commandery officials and the great prefectures referred cases to the Commandant of Trials, reaching over a thousand cases in a single year.
translations.idiomatic: Commandery officials and the great prefectures referred cases to the Commandant of Trials, and the number reached over a thousand in a single year.

sentences.zh: 章大者連逮證案數百，小者數十人；

translations.literal: The major cases involved the arrest and investigation of several hundred people connected to them; the minor ones, several dozen;
translations.idiomatic: In the major cases, several hundred people were arrested and investigated as connected parties; in the minor ones, several dozen;

sentences.zh: 遠者數千，近者數百里。

translations.literal: those summoned came from as far as several thousand li, and the nearest from several hundred li away.
translations.idiomatic: suspects were summoned from as far as several thousand li away, and the nearest from several hundred.

sentences.zh: 會獄，吏因責如章告劾，不服，以笞掠定之。

translations.literal: When the prisoners were gathered for trial, the officials would press charges in accordance with the accusations; if anyone refused to confess, they were beaten until they submitted.
translations.idiomatic: When the prisoners were gathered for trial, the officials pressed charges according to the original accusations. If anyone refused to confess, they were beaten and flogged until they submitted.

sentences.zh: 於是聞有逮皆亡匿。

translations.literal: Thereupon, all who heard of an impending arrest would flee and go into hiding.
translations.idiomatic: And so, anyone who heard that arrests were coming would flee and go into hiding.

sentences.zh: 獄久者至更數赦十有餘歲而相告言，大抵盡詆以不道以上。

translations.literal: Those imprisoned for long periods endured through several amnesties over more than ten years, and continued to denounce one another; most were ultimately charged with crimes of 'not following the Way' or above.
translations.idiomatic: The longest-held prisoners languished through several amnesties over more than ten years, continuing to denounce one another. Most were ultimately charged with crimes of "violating the Way" or worse.

sentences.zh: 廷尉及中都官詔獄逮至六七萬人，吏所增加十萬餘人。

translations.literal: The Commandant of Trials and the central government offices arrested sixty to seventy thousand people on imperial decree; those added by officials brought the total to over a hundred thousand.
translations.idiomatic: The Commandant of Trials and the central government offices arrested sixty to seventy thousand people under imperial decree; supplementary arrests by officials swelled the total to over a hundred thousand.

sentences.zh: 周中廢，後為執金時，逐盜，捕治桑弘羊、衛皇后昆弟子刻深，天子以為盡力無私，遷為御史大夫。

translations.literal: Zhou was once dismissed, but later served as Commandant of the Palace Guard. While pursuing bandits, he arrested and harshly investigated Sang Hongyang and relatives of Empress Wei's brothers; the emperor considered him to be a man who gave his utmost without personal bias, and promoted him to Imperial Secretary.
translations.idiomatic: Du Zhou was dismissed at one point, but later served as Commandant of the Palace Guard. While pursuing bandits, he arrested and harshly investigated Sang Hongyang and the nephews of Empress Wei's brothers. The emperor considered him a man who gave his utmost without personal bias, and promoted him to the rank of Imperial Secretary.

sentences.zh: 家兩子，夾河為守。

translations.literal: His two sons each served as governor on either side of the Yellow River.
translations.idiomatic: His two sons each served as governor, one on either side of the Yellow River.

sentences.zh: 其治暴酷皆甚於王溫舒等矣。

translations.literal: Their cruel and violent governance surpassed that of Wang Wenshu and the others.
translations.idiomatic: Their brutality and cruelty in governance surpassed even that of Wang Wenshu and his like.

sentences.zh: 杜周初徵為廷史，有一馬，且不全；

translations.literal: When Du Zhou was first summoned to serve as a court clerk, he had but a single horse, and even that was not sound;
translations.idiomatic: When Du Zhou was first summoned to serve as a court clerk, he owned but a single horse, and even that one was not sound;

sentences.zh: 及身久任事，至三公列，子孫尊官，家訾累數巨萬矣。

translations.literal: by the time he had long served in office and risen to the rank of the Three Excellencies, his sons and grandsons held high positions, and his family's wealth had accumulated to tens of millions.
translations.idiomatic: yet by the time he had risen through long service to the rank of the Three Excellencies, his sons and grandsons held high positions, and his family's wealth had accumulated to tens of millions.

sentences.zh: 太史公曰：自郅都、杜周十人者，此皆以酷烈為聲。

translations.literal: The Grand Historian says: From Zhi Du to Du Zhou, these ten men all gained their reputations through harshness and severity.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Historian remarks: From Zhi Du to Du Zhou, all ten of these men built their reputations on harshness and severity.

sentences.zh: 然郅都伉直，引是非，爭天下大體。

translations.literal: Yet Zhi Du was forthright and upright, distinguishing right from wrong, contending for the greater principles of the realm.
translations.idiomatic: Yet Zhi Du was forthright and upright, distinguishing right from wrong, and contended for the greater principles of the realm.

sentences.zh: 張湯以知陰陽，人主與俱上下，時數辯當否，國家賴其便。

translations.literal: Zhang Tang, understanding the interplay of yin and yang, rose and fell together with the ruler, frequently debating the propriety of measures; the state benefited from his capabilities.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Tang understood the subtle interplay of light and shadow, rising and falling in step with the sovereign, frequently debating the merits and faults of state policy. The empire benefited from his competence.

sentences.zh: 趙禹時據法守正。

translations.literal: Zhao Yu in his time upheld the law and maintained rectitude.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Yu, for his part, held fast to the law and maintained his integrity.

sentences.zh: 杜周從諛，以少言為重。

translations.literal: Du Zhou followed along with flattery, and cultivated an air of gravity through speaking little.
translations.idiomatic: Du Zhou was a sycophant who cultivated an air of gravity by speaking little.

sentences.zh: 自張湯死后，網密，多詆嚴，官事寖以秏廢。

translations.literal: After Zhang Tang's death, the net of law grew tighter, accusations and penalties harsher, and the conduct of government gradually fell into decline and waste.
translations.idiomatic: After Zhang Tang's death, the net of law grew ever tighter, accusations and penalties ever harsher, and the business of government gradually fell into decay and ruin.

sentences.zh: 九卿碌碌┐其官，救過不贍，何暇論繩墨之外乎！

translations.literal: The Nine Ministers toiled away in their offices, barely managing to cover their own errors; how could they have leisure to concern themselves with matters beyond the measuring line!
translations.idiomatic: The Nine Ministers toiled away in their posts, barely managing to cover their own errors. How could they have found the leisure to concern themselves with anything beyond the strict letter of the law!

sentences.zh: 然此十人中，其廉者足以為儀表，其污者足以為戒，方略教導，禁姦止邪，一切亦皆彬彬質有其文武焉。

translations.literal: Yet among these ten, those who were upright served well as models of conduct, and those who were corrupt served well as cautionary examples. In their strategies and guidance, in their prohibition of wickedness and suppression of evil, all of them without exception displayed a refined balance of the civil and martial virtues.
translations.idiomatic: Yet among these ten men, the upright ones served as worthy models, and the corrupt as cautionary examples. In their strategies of governance, their guidance and instruction, their suppression of wickedness and prohibition of evil, every one of them displayed, in their own way, a refined balance of the civil and martial virtues.

sentences.zh: 雖慘酷，斯稱其位矣。

translations.literal: Though harsh and cruel, they were worthy of their positions.
translations.idiomatic: Though they were harsh and cruel, they proved themselves worthy of the positions they held.

sentences.zh: 至若蜀守馮當暴挫，廣漢李貞擅磔人，東郡彌仆鋸項，天水駱璧推咸，河東褚廣妄殺，京兆無忌、馮翊殷周蝮鷙，水衡閻奉樸擊賣請，何足數哉！

translations.literal: As for Feng Dang, Governor of Shu, who brutally crushed the people; Li Zhen of Guanghan, who took it upon himself to have men dismembered; Mi Pu of Dongjun, who sawed through necks; Luo Bi of Tianshui, who tortured all under interrogation; Chu Guang of Hedong, who killed recklessly; Wu Ji of Jingzhao and Yin Zhou of Fengyi, who were as venomous as vipers and as fierce as hawks; and Yan Feng the Comptroller of Waters, who extorted confessions through beatings — are they even worth enumerating!
translations.idiomatic: As for Feng Dang, Governor of Shu, who brutally crushed the people; Li Zhen of Guanghan, who took it upon himself to dismember prisoners; Mi Pu of Dongjun, who sawed through necks; Luo Bi of Tianshui, who tortured all he interrogated; Chu Guang of Hedong, who killed without cause; Wu Ji of Jingzhao and Yin Zhou of Fengyi, venomous as vipers and fierce as hawks; and Yan Feng the Comptroller of Waters, who extorted confessions through beatings — are they even worth enumerating!

sentences.zh: 何足數哉！

translations.literal: How are they worth enumerating!
translations.idiomatic: They are not even worth enumerating!
